phase4_verified_raw: misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: verifySafeHtml() always derives and opens an English bundle file (englishFile) and throws RuntimeException if it can’t be opened. This will crash verification (and thus the build/tooling) for any locale file without a corresponding derived _en.properties at that path, or for files not matching the expected naming convention.

misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: verifySafeHtml() computes englishFile via file.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties", "_en.properties"). The character class [a-zA-Z-_] contains an unintended range (Z-_) due to the '-' placement, so it can match unexpected characters and rewrite the path incorrectly (or fail to match), leading to reading the wrong file or throwing at runtime for valid filenames.

misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: santizeAnchors() mutates value using value.replaceFirst(Pattern.quote(englishMatcher.group()), "") while iterating matches from matcher over the original value. If the same anchor appears multiple times or the first occurrence isn’t the current matcher occurrence, replaceFirst can remove the wrong instance, causing subsequent comparisons to fail and producing false "Didn't find anchor tag" errors.

misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: containsHtml() uses Pattern HTML_TAGS = Pattern.compile("<[a-z]+[^>]*>") which is case-sensitive and only matches lowercase tag names. If the English source contains allowed HTML with uppercase/mixed-case tags (e.g.,
), containsHtml() returns false and the translation is validated with POLICY_NO_HTML, incorrectly flagging valid HTML as illegal.

misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: method name typo "santizeAnchors" (missing 'i' in sanitize). This is a real identifier typo that can break/refuse expected usage by callers and makes the API misleading/fragile for future maintenance.
phase4_final: misc/theme-verifier/src/main/java/org/keycloak/themeverifier/VerifyMessageProperties.java: verifySafeHtml() always derives and opens an English bundle file (englishFile) and throws RuntimeException if it can’t be opened. This will crash verification (and thus the build/tooling) for any locale file without a corresponding derived _en.properties at that path, or for files not matching the expected naming convention.

phase4_verified_raw: testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AssertEvents.java: AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId(String) has inverted matcher logic. It does `if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;` which rejects token IDs when the grant shortcut matches, so events expecting a specific grant shortcut will fail for valid token IDs.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AssertEvents.java: AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId(String) extracts the grant shortcut with wrong substring indices. Comment/encoding imply context is 6 chars: st[0-1], tt[2-3], gt[4-5], so grant shortcut should be `substring(4, 6)`, but code uses `substring(3, 5)` (last char of tokenType + first char of grantType), causing mismatches and potential StringIndexOutOfBoundsException for short prefixes.

services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/AccessTokenContext.java: AccessTokenContext constructor null-checks the wrong variable. It calls `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed")` instead of checking `rawTokenId`, allowing rawTokenId to be null and later producing encoded token IDs ending with ":null" or causing downstream NPEs.

services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory.java: postInit() duplicate-shortcut validation is ineffective. It checks `grantsByShortcuts.size() != grantsToShortcuts.size()`, but both maps can end up the same size even when two different grants share the same shortcut (overwrites in both maps), so duplicated shortcuts can slip through and cause grantType decoding/encoding collisions at runtime.
phase4_final: testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/AssertEvents.java: AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId(String) has inverted matcher logic. It does `if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;` which rejects token IDs when the grant shortcut matches, so events expecting a specific grant shortcut will fail for valid token IDs.

services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/AccessTokenContext.java: AccessTokenContext constructor null-checks the wrong variable. It calls `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed")` instead of checking `rawTokenId`, allowing rawTokenId to be null and later producing encoded token IDs ending with ":null" or causing downstream NPEs.

services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/DefaultTokenContextEncoderProviderFactory.java: postInit() duplicate-shortcut validation is ineffective. It checks `grantsByShortcuts.size() != grantsToShortcuts.size()`, but both maps can end up the same size even when two different grants share the same shortcut (overwrites in both maps), so duplicated shortcuts can slip through and cause grantType decoding/encoding collisions at runtime.

phase4_verified_raw: services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/model/RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean.java: RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean() calls credentialModelOpt.get() without checking presence (RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user) can return Optional.empty()). This will throw NoSuchElementException and crash login page rendering for users without recovery-codes credential.

server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CredentialHelper.java: createRecoveryCodesCredential() serializes the full List<String> of generated codes to JSON and passes it as UserCredentialModel.value, then calls user.credentialManager().updateCredential(recoveryCodesCredential). UserStorageProvider.updateCredential (including BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage in this PR) persists input.getChallengeResponse() as credentialData, so it stores the entire JSON array string. Later validation compares each stored code to a single entered code, so recovery-code login will fail for user-storage/federated users.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/servers/auth-server/services/testsuite-providers/src/main/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/federation/BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.java: updateCredential() for RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.TYPE stores input.getChallengeResponse() directly into credentialData. With this PR’s createRecoveryCodesCredential() path, that value is the JSON array string, not a single code, so isValid() will never match a single entered code.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/servers/auth-server/services/testsuite-providers/src/main/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/federation/BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.java: isValid() deserializes storedRecoveryKeys.getCredentialData() into a raw List and compares elements via equals() to input.getChallengeResponse(). If credentialData is the JSON array string (as stored by updateCredential in this PR), deserialization will not yield the expected List<String> of codes (or may fail), and the equals comparison against the single entered code will never succeed, breaking recovery-code login.

server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.java: getCredential(UserModel) always prefers getFederatedCredentialsStream() over local stored credentials. If both exist, it will select the federated one even if stale/invalid, causing recovery-code selection/verification to operate on the wrong credential.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/federation/storage/BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorageTest.java: configureBrowserFlowWithRecoveryAuthnCodes() sets config alias to "delayed-suthenticator-config" (typo). This prevents the delayed-authenticator execution from receiving the intended config (or breaks lookup by alias), so the flow behavior under test is not what the test intends.
phase4_final: services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/model/RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean.java: RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean() calls credentialModelOpt.get() without checking presence (RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user) can return Optional.empty()). This will throw NoSuchElementException and crash login page rendering for users without recovery-codes credential.

server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/utils/CredentialHelper.java: createRecoveryCodesCredential() serializes the full List<String> of generated codes to JSON and passes it as UserCredentialModel.value, then calls user.credentialManager().updateCredential(recoveryCodesCredential). UserStorageProvider.updateCredential (including BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage in this PR) persists input.getChallengeResponse() as credentialData, so it stores the entire JSON array string. Later validation compares each stored code to a single entered code, so recovery-code login will fail for user-storage/federated users.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/servers/auth-server/services/testsuite-providers/src/main/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/federation/BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorage.java: updateCredential() for RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.TYPE stores input.getChallengeResponse() directly into credentialData. With this PR’s createRecoveryCodesCredential() path, that value is the JSON array string, not a single code, so isValid() will never match a single entered code.

server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.java: getCredential(UserModel) always prefers getFederatedCredentialsStream() over local stored credentials. If both exist, it will select the federated one even if stale/invalid, causing recovery-code selection/verification to operate on the wrong credential.

testsuite/integration-arquillian/tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/federation/storage/BackwardsCompatibilityUserStorageTest.java: configureBrowserFlowWithRecoveryAuthnCodes() sets config alias to "delayed-suthenticator-config" (typo). This prevents the delayed-authenticator execution from receiving the intended config (or breaks lookup by alias), so the flow behavior under test is not what the test intends.

phase4_verified_raw: quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/compatibility/CompatibilityResult.java: API/contract break — RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE changed from 4 to 3 and 4 is now FEATURE_DISABLED. This will cause wrong behavior for any existing automation/tests/scripts that interpret exit code 4 as 'recreate upgrade required' (now misread as 'feature disabled').

quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/cli/command/UpdateCompatibilityCheck.java and UpdateCompatibilityMetadata.java: new early-exit uses picocli.exit(CompatibilityResult.FEATURE_DISABLED) (exit code 4). Combined with the changed RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE mapping, callers relying on previous exit-code semantics will mis-handle outcomes (recreate-required vs feature-disabled).
phase4_final: quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/compatibility/CompatibilityResult.java: API/contract break — RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE changed from 4 to 3 and 4 is now FEATURE_DISABLED. This will cause wrong behavior for any existing automation/tests/scripts that interpret exit code 4 as 'recreate upgrade required' (now misread as 'feature disabled').

quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/cli/command/UpdateCompatibilityCheck.java and UpdateCompatibilityMetadata.java: new early-exit uses picocli.exit(CompatibilityResult.FEATURE_DISABLED) (exit code 4). Combined with the changed RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE mapping, callers relying on previous exit-code semantics will mis-handle outcomes (recreate-required vs feature-disabled).

phase4_verified_raw: AdminPermissions.registerListener (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/AdminPermissions.java): Listener execution is now gated by Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ. This PR introduces/uses V2 (MgmtPermissionsV2/ClientPermissionsV2) but does not gate the listener on ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2. If only the V2 flag is enabled, RoleRemoved/ClientRemoved/GroupRemoved events will no longer call setPermissionsEnabled(..., false), leaving stale authz resources/policies and causing incorrect permission evaluation.

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel,String) (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/ClientPermissionsV2.java): NullPointerException when falling back to the all-clients resource. If per-client resource is missing, it sets resource = AdminPermissionsSchema.SCHEMA.getResourceTypeResource(...). That can return null, but the code unconditionally calls authz.getStoreFactory().getPolicyStore().findByResource(server, resource) and later new ResourcePermission(resource, ...), both of which will crash when resource is null.

UserResourceTypeEvaluationTest.testImpersonatePermission (tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/tests/admin/authz/fgap/UserResourceTypeEvaluationTest.java): createPermission is called with userAlice.getId() as the resource id, but userAlice is a ManagedUser (not a representation) and elsewhere in the same file the correct pattern is userAlice.admin().toRepresentation().getId() or userAlice.getId() only if ManagedUser exposes it. In this diff, earlier lines use userAlice.admin().toRepresentation().getId(), so this is a likely compile error / method-not-found at build time for ManagedUser.getId().
phase4_final: AdminPermissions.registerListener (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/AdminPermissions.java): Listener execution is now gated by Profile.Feature.ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ. This PR introduces/uses V2 (MgmtPermissionsV2/ClientPermissionsV2) but does not gate the listener on ADMIN_FINE_GRAINED_AUTHZ_V2. If only the V2 flag is enabled, RoleRemoved/ClientRemoved/GroupRemoved events will no longer call setPermissionsEnabled(..., false), leaving stale authz resources/policies and causing incorrect permission evaluation.

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(ClientModel,String) (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/ClientPermissionsV2.java): NullPointerException when falling back to the all-clients resource. If per-client resource is missing, it sets resource = AdminPermissionsSchema.SCHEMA.getResourceTypeResource(...). That can return null, but the code unconditionally calls authz.getStoreFactory().getPolicyStore().findByResource(server, resource) and later new ResourcePermission(resource, ...), both of which will crash when resource is null.

UserResourceTypeEvaluationTest.testImpersonatePermission (tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/tests/admin/authz/fgap/UserResourceTypeEvaluationTest.java): createPermission is called with userAlice.getId() as the resource id, but userAlice is a ManagedUser (not a representation) and elsewhere in the same file the correct pattern is userAlice.admin().toRepresentation().getId() or userAlice.getId() only if ManagedUser exposes it. In this diff, earlier lines use userAlice.admin().toRepresentation().getId(), so this is a likely compile error / method-not-found at build time for ManagedUser.getId().

phase4_verified_raw: UsersResource.searchForUser (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java): removed per-user permission filter `.filter(usersEvaluator::canView)` and now passes the raw `session.users().searchForUserStream(...)` into `toRepresentation(...)`, which can return representations for users the caller is not allowed to view (authorization bypass/data exposure).

BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser (rest/admin-ui-ext/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/ui/rest/BruteForceUsersResource.java): same regression as UsersResource—removed per-user permission filter and now converts the raw `searchForUserStream(...)` results to representations without `usersEvaluator::canView`, potentially exposing unauthorized users.

UsersResource.searchForUser and BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser: the previous `if (!auth.users().canView())` guard was removed and the code now conditionally sets `session.setAttribute(UserModel.GROUPS, groupIds)` but never clears it when `groupIds` is empty; since `getGroupIdsWithViewPermission()` explicitly returns an empty set when the caller can view globally, a stale `UserModel.GROUPS` session attribute from a prior restricted call can incorrectly restrict later searches in the same session (wrong results/cross-request leakage).

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/GroupPermissionsV2.java): wrong scopes checked—returns `hasPermission(null, VIEW, MANAGE)` so a caller with only VIEW permission will pass `canManage()` checks, enabling group-management operations that should require MANAGE.

GroupPermissionsV2.getGroupIdsWithViewPermission (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/GroupPermissionsV2.java): identifier mismatch—iterates `resourceStore.findByType(..., GROUPS_RESOURCE_TYPE, ...)` and calls `hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...)`, but `hasPermission` resolves per-group resources via `resourceStore.findByName(server, groupId)` (expects resource name == groupId). Passing the internal resource id makes `findByName` fail and forces fallback to the all-groups resource, so per-group VIEW_MEMBERS/MANAGE_MEMBERS grants are not detected and the returned set can be empty/incorrect, breaking group-scoped user search restrictions.
phase4_final: UsersResource.searchForUser (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/UsersResource.java): removed per-user permission filter `.filter(usersEvaluator::canView)` and now passes the raw `session.users().searchForUserStream(...)` into `toRepresentation(...)`, which can return representations for users the caller is not allowed to view (authorization bypass/data exposure).

BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser (rest/admin-ui-ext/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/ui/rest/BruteForceUsersResource.java): same regression as UsersResource—removed per-user permission filter and now converts the raw `searchForUserStream(...)` results to representations without `usersEvaluator::canView`, potentially exposing unauthorized users.

UsersResource.searchForUser and BruteForceUsersResource.searchForUser: the previous `if (!auth.users().canView())` guard was removed and the code now conditionally sets `session.setAttribute(UserModel.GROUPS, groupIds)` but never clears it when `groupIds` is empty; since `getGroupIdsWithViewPermission()` explicitly returns an empty set when the caller can view globally, a stale `UserModel.GROUPS` session attribute from a prior restricted call can incorrectly restrict later searches in the same session (wrong results/cross-request leakage).

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/GroupPermissionsV2.java): wrong scopes checked—returns `hasPermission(null, VIEW, MANAGE)` so a caller with only VIEW permission will pass `canManage()` checks, enabling group-management operations that should require MANAGE.

GroupPermissionsV2.getGroupIdsWithViewPermission (services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/permissions/GroupPermissionsV2.java): identifier mismatch—iterates `resourceStore.findByType(..., GROUPS_RESOURCE_TYPE, ...)` and calls `hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...)`, but `hasPermission` resolves per-group resources via `resourceStore.findByName(server, groupId)` (expects resource name == groupId). Passing the internal resource id makes `findByName` fail and forces fallback to the all-groups resource, so per-group VIEW_MEMBERS/MANAGE_MEMBERS grants are not detected and the returned set can be empty/incorrect, breaking group-scoped user search restrictions.

phase4_verified_raw: authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: readNext() miscomputes element length and corrupts stream position. It mark()s, reads tag+tagNumber+length, then does `length += reset()` (where reset() returns bytes already read), and finally `read(length)`. This re-reads the header bytes plus content, so the returned element is missing the initial header and the underlying stream advances too far, breaking subsequent parsing.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: mark() uses `is.mark(is.available())` as readlimit. `available()` is not a safe/readlimit guarantee for mark/reset semantics and can be 0; after reading any bytes, reset() may throw (invalid mark), causing readNext()/readSequence() to fail on some streams/inputs.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: readSequence() does not handle indefinite-length encoding. readLength() can return -1, but the loop is `while (length > 0)`, so for indefinite-length SEQUENCE it returns an empty list instead of reading until end-of-contents, breaking decoding.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: if readLength() returns -1 (indefinite length), readNext() does `length += reset()`, turning -1 into a small positive number (headerBytes-1) and then reads only that many bytes, truncating the element and causing incorrect parsing downstream.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: readSequence() subtracts `bytes.length` from `length`, but readNext() returns the full element length (tag+len+value) while `length` is the SEQUENCE content length. This makes `length` underflow/terminate early and truncates parsing for valid DER sequences.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/AuthzClientCryptoProvider.java: getBouncyCastleProvider() returns the Provider of the default KeyStore type (`KeyStore.getInstance(KeyStore.getDefaultType()).getProvider()`), which is typically a JDK provider (e.g., SUN) and not BouncyCastle/BCFIPS. This violates the method contract/name and can cause algorithm/provider mismatches at runtime when callers expect BC/BCFIPS-specific behavior.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/AuthzClientCryptoProvider.java: getKeyStore(KeystoreUtil.KeystoreFormat format) uses `KeyStore.getInstance(format.name())`. This is not equivalent to the existing providers (e.g., FIPS1402Provider uses `format.toString()` with provider mapping) and will throw KeyStoreException at runtime if enum names don’t match valid KeyStore type strings for all supported formats.

common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/crypto/CryptoProvider.java: adding new abstract method `int order()` is an API compatibility break. Any existing CryptoProvider implementations not updated in this PR will fail to compile, or can throw AbstractMethodError at runtime when loaded via ServiceLoader and invoked by CryptoIntegration.detectProvider() sorting.
phase4_final: authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/ASN1Decoder.java: readNext() miscomputes element length and corrupts stream position. It mark()s, reads tag+tagNumber+length, then does `length += reset()` (where reset() returns bytes already read), and finally `read(length)`. This re-reads the header bytes plus content, so the returned element is missing the initial header and the underlying stream advances too far, breaking subsequent parsing.

authz/client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/client/util/crypto/AuthzClientCryptoProvider.java: getBouncyCastleProvider() returns the Provider of the default KeyStore type (`KeyStore.getInstance(KeyStore.getDefaultType()).getProvider()`), which is typically a JDK provider (e.g., SUN) and not BouncyCastle/BCFIPS. This violates the method contract/name and can cause algorithm/provider mismatches at runtime when callers expect BC/BCFIPS-specific behavior.

common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/crypto/CryptoProvider.java: adding new abstract method `int order()` is an API compatibility break. Any existing CryptoProvider implementations not updated in this PR will fail to compile, or can throw AbstractMethodError at runtime when loaded via ServiceLoader and invoked by CryptoIntegration.detectProvider() sorting.

phase4_verified_raw: model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/GroupAdapter.java: getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null ("return model == null ? null : model.getSubGroupsCount();"). This is a concrete behavior change that can cause NullPointerException via autounboxing to long or break callers that assume a numeric count (previously it always dereferenced the model and would not return null).

tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/tests/admin/group/GroupTest.java: createMultiDeleteMultiReadMulti() starts a reader thread but never joins/stops it deterministically before asserting. The assertion on caughtExceptions can run before the reader thread has a chance to record exceptions, making the test nondeterministically pass (false negative) and leaving a background thread running beyond the test.
phase4_final: model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/GroupAdapter.java: getSubGroupsCount() now returns null when modelSupplier.get() returns null ("return model == null ? null : model.getSubGroupsCount();"). This is a concrete behavior change that can cause NullPointerException via autounboxing to long or break callers that assume a numeric count (previously it always dereferenced the model and would not return null).

tests/base/src/test/java/org/keycloak/tests/admin/group/GroupTest.java: createMultiDeleteMultiReadMulti() starts a reader thread but never joins/stops it deterministically before asserting. The assertion on caughtExceptions can run before the reader thread has a chance to record exceptions, making the test nondeterministically pass (false negative) and leaving a background thread running beyond the test.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.__init__ uses `self.max_processes = max_processes or len(buffer.assigned_shards)` without guarding `max_processes==0`; passing `--flusher-processes 0` (allowed by click type=int) will be treated as falsy and silently replaced with `len(buffer.assigned_shards)`, violating the CLI contract and preventing users from disabling/limiting processes to 0 as specified.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher._create_process_for_shards constructs `shard_buffer = SpansBuffer(shards)` positionally, but elsewhere in the codebase (per PR description) SpansBuffer is constructed with `assigned_shards=[...]`; if SpansBuffer.__init__ expects `assigned_shards` (or a different positional signature), this will raise at runtime or misconfigure shard assignment, preventing flusher startup for all shards.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.main uses inconsistent metric tag keys for the same shard identifier: `metrics.timer('spans.buffer.flusher.produce', tags={'shard': shard_tag})` vs `metrics.timer('spans.buffer.flusher.wait_produce', tags={'shards': shard_tag})`, which will split metrics across two dimensions and break shard-level dashboards/alerts.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.join computes `remaining_time = deadline - time.time()` but never uses it to bound the per-process wait; it waits each process until the global deadline, then unconditionally calls `process.terminate()` for every multiprocessing.Process even if it exited cleanly or is still within the allowed timeout window, risking premature termination and loss of in-flight flush work.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher._ensure_processes_alive restart cap is off-by-one: it checks `if self.process_restarts[process_index] > MAX_PROCESS_RESTARTS:` before incrementing, then increments and restarts, allowing `MAX_PROCESS_RESTARTS + 1` restarts before raising, contrary to the intended cap.
phase4_final: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.__init__ uses `self.max_processes = max_processes or len(buffer.assigned_shards)` without guarding `max_processes==0`; passing `--flusher-processes 0` (allowed by click type=int) will be treated as falsy and silently replaced with `len(buffer.assigned_shards)`, violating the CLI contract and preventing users from disabling/limiting processes to 0 as specified.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher._create_process_for_shards constructs `shard_buffer = SpansBuffer(shards)` positionally, but elsewhere in the codebase (per PR description) SpansBuffer is constructed with `assigned_shards=[...]`; if SpansBuffer.__init__ expects `assigned_shards` (or a different positional signature), this will raise at runtime or misconfigure shard assignment, preventing flusher startup for all shards.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.main uses inconsistent metric tag keys for the same shard identifier: `metrics.timer('spans.buffer.flusher.produce', tags={'shard': shard_tag})` vs `metrics.timer('spans.buffer.flusher.wait_produce', tags={'shards': shard_tag})`, which will split metrics across two dimensions and break shard-level dashboards/alerts.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher.join computes `remaining_time = deadline - time.time()` but never uses it to bound the per-process wait; it waits each process until the global deadline, then unconditionally calls `process.terminate()` for every multiprocessing.Process even if it exited cleanly or is still within the allowed timeout window, risking premature termination and loss of in-flight flush work.

src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: SpanFlusher._ensure_processes_alive restart cap is off-by-one: it checks `if self.process_restarts[process_index] > MAX_PROCESS_RESTARTS:` before incrementing, then increments and restarts, allowing `MAX_PROCESS_RESTARTS + 1` restarts before raising, contrary to the intended cap.

phase4_verified_raw: static/app/views/explore/hooks/useTraceItemAttributeKeys.tsx useTraceItemAttributeKeys: returns `attributes: isFetching ? previous : data` where both `data` (react-query initial state) and `previous` can be `undefined`, regressing from always returning a `TagCollection` object; consumers doing `Object.keys(attributes)` / spreading will crash with `Cannot convert undefined or null to object`.

src/sentry/replays/endpoints/project_replay_summarize_breadcrumbs.py fetch_error_details: pairs `error_ids` with `events.values()` via `zip(error_ids, events.values())` after `nodestore.backend.get_multi(node_ids)`; dict `.values()` order is not guaranteed to match `error_ids/node_ids`, so error details can be attached to the wrong `event_id` (wrong title/message/timestamp in summaries).

src/sentry/replays/endpoints/project_replay_summarize_breadcrumbs.py: new code uses `json.loads(...)` and `json.dumps(...)` but the module no longer imports `json` (only `functools`, `logging`, etc.); this will raise `NameError: name 'json' is not defined` at runtime in `gen_request_data` and `analyze_recording_segments`.

static/app/views/dashboards/widgetCard/chart.tsx WidgetCardChart.tableResultComponent: when feature `use-table-widget-visualization` is enabled, it renders `TableWidgetVisualization` with hardcoded empty `columns={[]}` and `tableData` containing empty `data`/`meta.fields`/`meta.units`, ignoring `result.data` and `result.meta`; this will always show an empty table instead of the actual query results (wrong behavior).

static/app/views/dashboards/widgets/tableWidget/tableWidgetVisualization.tsx TableWidgetVisualization: computes `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields)` without guarding `tableData.meta`/`tableData.meta.fields`; if `tableData.meta` is undefined, this throws at runtime (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'fields')`).
phase4_final: static/app/views/explore/hooks/useTraceItemAttributeKeys.tsx useTraceItemAttributeKeys: returns `attributes: isFetching ? previous : data` where both `data` (react-query initial state) and `previous` can be `undefined`, regressing from always returning a `TagCollection` object; consumers doing `Object.keys(attributes)` / spreading will crash with `Cannot convert undefined or null to object`.

src/sentry/replays/endpoints/project_replay_summarize_breadcrumbs.py fetch_error_details: pairs `error_ids` with `events.values()` via `zip(error_ids, events.values())` after `nodestore.backend.get_multi(node_ids)`; dict `.values()` order is not guaranteed to match `error_ids/node_ids`, so error details can be attached to the wrong `event_id` (wrong title/message/timestamp in summaries).

src/sentry/replays/endpoints/project_replay_summarize_breadcrumbs.py: new code uses `json.loads(...)` and `json.dumps(...)` but the module no longer imports `json` (only `functools`, `logging`, etc.); this will raise `NameError: name 'json' is not defined` at runtime in `gen_request_data` and `analyze_recording_segments`.

static/app/views/dashboards/widgetCard/chart.tsx WidgetCardChart.tableResultComponent: when feature `use-table-widget-visualization` is enabled, it renders `TableWidgetVisualization` with hardcoded empty `columns={[]}` and `tableData` containing empty `data`/`meta.fields`/`meta.units`, ignoring `result.data` and `result.meta`; this will always show an empty table instead of the actual query results (wrong behavior).

static/app/views/dashboards/widgets/tableWidget/tableWidgetVisualization.tsx TableWidgetVisualization: computes `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields)` without guarding `tableData.meta`/`tableData.meta.fields`; if `tableData.meta` is undefined, this throws at runtime (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'fields')`).

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/api/paginator.py:get_result (existing paginator): start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset can be negative when cursor.is_prev is True and cursor.offset < 0; results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]) will raise at runtime because Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative start indices (AssertionError: Negative indexing is not supported).

src/sentry/api/paginator.py:OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result: when enable_advanced_features is True and cursor.offset < 0, it executes results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]) with start_offset = cursor.offset (negative); Django QuerySet slicing with negative indices raises AssertionError, so this branch will crash for negative offsets.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py:OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key: value = getattr(item, self.key) is used with order_by='-datetime' from organization_auditlogs.py, so self.key will be 'datetime' and value is a datetime; math.floor/ceil on a datetime raises TypeError, causing optimized pagination to crash when building cursors.

src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py:get: enable_advanced = request.user.is_superuser or organization_context.member.has_global_access can crash if organization_context.member is None (e.g., request user not a member / member lookup failed); this is a new null-safety issue introduced by the optimized pagination gating logic.
phase4_final: src/sentry/api/paginator.py:get_result (existing paginator): start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset can be negative when cursor.is_prev is True and cursor.offset < 0; results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]) will raise at runtime because Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative start indices (AssertionError: Negative indexing is not supported).

src/sentry/api/paginator.py:OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result: when enable_advanced_features is True and cursor.offset < 0, it executes results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]) with start_offset = cursor.offset (negative); Django QuerySet slicing with negative indices raises AssertionError, so this branch will crash for negative offsets.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py:OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key: value = getattr(item, self.key) is used with order_by='-datetime' from organization_auditlogs.py, so self.key will be 'datetime' and value is a datetime; math.floor/ceil on a datetime raises TypeError, causing optimized pagination to crash when building cursors.

src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py:get: enable_advanced = request.user.is_superuser or organization_context.member.has_global_access can crash if organization_context.member is None (e.g., request user not a member / member lookup failed); this is a new null-safety issue introduced by the optimized pagination gating logic.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/incidents/grouptype.py: MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but is just `pass` and does not implement required abstract members (e.g., counter_names/get_dedupe_value/get_group_key_values/build_occurrence_and_event_data). Instantiating it will raise `TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class ...`, breaking any code path that tries to use this handler via GroupType.detector_handler.

src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py: process_detectors() now assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and iterates `detector_results.values()`, but DetectorHandler.evaluate is still abstract and may have existing implementations returning a list. Any non-updated handler returning a list will crash with `AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'values'` at runtime.

src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py: StatefulDetectorHandler.evaluate_group_key_value() calls `PriorityLevel(new_status)` where `new_status` is a DetectorPriorityLevel enum. This is a concrete type mismatch; if PriorityLevel is not constructible from a DetectorPriorityLevel instance, it will raise (ValueError/TypeError) and prevent occurrence creation on non-OK transitions. The diff provides no conversion via `.value` or mapping.

tests/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/test_detector.py: test_state_results_multi_group builds the second expected occurrence with the wrong value (`build_mock_occurrence_and_event(..., "group_2", 6, ...)` even though group_2 input value is 10). This makes the test assert the wrong behavior and can mask real bugs in per-group value handling.
phase4_final: src/sentry/incidents/grouptype.py: MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but is just `pass` and does not implement required abstract members (e.g., counter_names/get_dedupe_value/get_group_key_values/build_occurrence_and_event_data). Instantiating it will raise `TypeError: Can't instantiate abstract class ...`, breaking any code path that tries to use this handler via GroupType.detector_handler.

src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py: process_detectors() now assumes handler.evaluate() returns a dict and iterates `detector_results.values()`, but DetectorHandler.evaluate is still abstract and may have existing implementations returning a list. Any non-updated handler returning a list will crash with `AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'values'` at runtime.

src/sentry/workflow_engine/processors/detector.py: StatefulDetectorHandler.evaluate_group_key_value() calls `PriorityLevel(new_status)` where `new_status` is a DetectorPriorityLevel enum. This is a concrete type mismatch; if PriorityLevel is not constructible from a DetectorPriorityLevel instance, it will raise (ValueError/TypeError) and prevent occurrence creation on non-OK transitions. The diff provides no conversion via `.value` or mapping.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/monitors/logic/incident_occurrence.py:get_monitor_environment_context: Builds a modified local copy of monitor config in variable `config` (e.g., converting `schedule_type` to display), but returns `"config": monitor_environment.monitor.config` instead of the modified `config`, so the occurrence context emits the raw/unmodified config.

src/sentry/monitors/logic/incident_occurrence.py:get_failure_reason: If `failed_checkins` contains no statuses present in `HUMAN_FAILURE_STATUS_MAP`, `status_counts` is empty; the code then builds `human_status` from an empty list and returns a message like " check-ins detected" (missing the problem description).

src/sentry/monitors/logic/incidents.py:try_incident_threshold: When `failure_issue_threshold > 1`, `previous_checkins` can be empty (e.g., no check-ins returned by the query); the code unconditionally does `starting_checkin = previous_checkins[0]`, which will raise IndexError and break incident creation.
phase4_final: src/sentry/monitors/logic/incident_occurrence.py:get_monitor_environment_context: Builds a modified local copy of monitor config in variable `config` (e.g., converting `schedule_type` to display), but returns `"config": monitor_environment.monitor.config` instead of the modified `config`, so the occurrence context emits the raw/unmodified config.

src/sentry/monitors/logic/incident_occurrence.py:get_failure_reason: If `failed_checkins` contains no statuses present in `HUMAN_FAILURE_STATUS_MAP`, `status_counts` is empty; the code then builds `human_status` from an empty list and returns a message like " check-ins detected" (missing the problem description).

src/sentry/monitors/logic/incidents.py:try_incident_threshold: When `failure_issue_threshold > 1`, `previous_checkins` can be empty (e.g., no check-ins returned by the query); the code unconditionally does `starting_checkin = previous_checkins[0]`, which will raise IndexError and break incident creation.

src/sentry/integrations/utils/sync.py: sync_group_assignee_inbound calls `user_service.get_many_by_email(emails=[email], ...)` without guarding `email` being None. If `email` is None, this passes `[None]` and can raise or return incorrect results, breaking inbound assignment sync for providers that omit email.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: OrderedQueueWorker.run catches `queue.ShutDown`, but the stdlib `queue` module has no `ShutDown` exception. This will raise `AttributeError` when the exception class is referenced, and the intended shutdown signaling via queue exceptions cannot work.

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: FixedQueuePool.shutdown calls `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` on `queue.Queue` instances. `queue.Queue` has no `shutdown` method, so this will raise `AttributeError` at runtime (it is caught and logged, but workers may remain blocked on `get()` and `join()` may time out, preventing graceful shutdown).

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: FixedQueuePool.get_queue_for_group uses Python built-in `hash(group_key) % self.num_queues` for “consistent hashing”. Python’s hash is randomized per process (PYTHONHASHSEED), so the same group_key will map to different queues across process restarts, breaking the stated guarantee that each group is consistently assigned to the same queue (ordering across restarts can be violated).

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/result_consumer.py: ResultsStrategyFactory.create_thread_queue_parallel_worker defines `commit_offsets(offsets)` and calls `commit(commit_data)` with only one argument, but Arroyo `Commit` callables are invoked as `commit(offsets, force=False)`; tests in this diff define `track_commits(offsets, force=False)`. This mismatch will raise `TypeError` at runtime and prevent committing offsets.

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy.submit calls `self.decoder(message.payload)`, but the code then asserts `message.value` is a `BrokerValue` and uses `message.value.partition/offset`, indicating the intended payload source is `message.value.payload`. If `Message` does not provide a `.payload` attribute (common is `.value.payload`), this will raise `AttributeError` and break processing.
phase4_final: src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: OrderedQueueWorker.run catches `queue.ShutDown`, but the stdlib `queue` module has no `ShutDown` exception. This will raise `AttributeError` when the exception class is referenced, and the intended shutdown signaling via queue exceptions cannot work.

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: FixedQueuePool.shutdown calls `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` on `queue.Queue` instances. `queue.Queue` has no `shutdown` method, so this will raise `AttributeError` at runtime (it is caught and logged, but workers may remain blocked on `get()` and `join()` may time out, preventing graceful shutdown).

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: FixedQueuePool.get_queue_for_group uses Python built-in `hash(group_key) % self.num_queues` for “consistent hashing”. Python’s hash is randomized per process (PYTHONHASHSEED), so the same group_key will map to different queues across process restarts, breaking the stated guarantee that each group is consistently assigned to the same queue (ordering across restarts can be violated).

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/result_consumer.py: ResultsStrategyFactory.create_thread_queue_parallel_worker defines `commit_offsets(offsets)` and calls `commit(commit_data)` with only one argument, but Arroyo `Commit` callables are invoked as `commit(offsets, force=False)`; tests in this diff define `track_commits(offsets, force=False)`. This mismatch will raise `TypeError` at runtime and prevent committing offsets.

src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py: SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy.submit calls `self.decoder(message.payload)`, but the code then asserts `message.value` is a `BrokerValue` and uses `message.value.partition/offset`, indicating the intended payload source is `message.value.payload`. If `Message` does not provide a `.payload` attribute (common is `.value.payload`), this will raise `AttributeError` and break processing.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/api/paginator.py: OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key calls math.floor/math.ceil on `value = getattr(item, self.key)`. In src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py this paginator is used with order_by='-datetime' (a datetime field), so floor/ceil on a datetime will raise TypeError and crash the audit logs endpoint when optimized_pagination=true and enable_advanced is true.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py: OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result and the modified BasePaginator.get_result both allow negative start_offset when cursor.is_prev is True (and OptimizedCursorPaginator explicitly allows negative offsets when enable_advanced_features and cursor.offset < 0). Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices; `queryset[start_offset:stop]` with a negative start will raise (AssertionError/ValueError), causing runtime crashes for negative-offset cursors.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py: Modified BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative offsets to 0 for non-prev cursors (`start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset`). This changes behavior for callers that pass a negative cursor.offset (now allowed per updated comment in src/sentry/utils/cursors.py) and will return the wrong page (restarts from beginning) rather than honoring the cursor offset semantics.
phase4_final: src/sentry/api/paginator.py: OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key calls math.floor/math.ceil on `value = getattr(item, self.key)`. In src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py this paginator is used with order_by='-datetime' (a datetime field), so floor/ceil on a datetime will raise TypeError and crash the audit logs endpoint when optimized_pagination=true and enable_advanced is true.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py: OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result and the modified BasePaginator.get_result both allow negative start_offset when cursor.is_prev is True (and OptimizedCursorPaginator explicitly allows negative offsets when enable_advanced_features and cursor.offset < 0). Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices; `queryset[start_offset:stop]` with a negative start will raise (AssertionError/ValueError), causing runtime crashes for negative-offset cursors.

src/sentry/api/paginator.py: Modified BasePaginator.get_result clamps negative offsets to 0 for non-prev cursors (`start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset`). This changes behavior for callers that pass a negative cursor.offset (now allowed per updated comment in src/sentry/utils/cursors.py) and will return the wrong page (restarts from beginning) rather than honoring the cursor offset semantics.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py:is_errors_query_for_error_upsampled_projects uses snuba_params.project_ids, but SnubaParams in the added tests is constructed with `projects=` and does not provide `project_ids`; this will raise AttributeError at runtime when organization_events_stats calls this helper.

src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py:is_errors_query_for_error_upsampled_projects and invalidate_upsampling_cache build cache keys with hash(tuple(sorted(...))). Python’s built-in hash() is randomized per process, so the same org/projects will produce different keys across workers, defeating cross-process caching and causing inconsistent cache hit behavior.

src/sentry/testutils/factories.py:_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling uses `if client_sample_rate:`; a valid client_sample_rate of 0/0.0/'0' will be treated as falsy and sample_rate will not be set, causing incorrect sampling metadata/behavior for events with an explicit 0 rate.

src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py:transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling rewrites `count()` to `upsampled_count() as count`; forcing alias `count` can collide with existing aliases/expressions when callers already alias count or have multiple yAxes, leading to Snuba query errors or ambiguous/duplicate column resolution.
phase4_final: src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py:is_errors_query_for_error_upsampled_projects uses snuba_params.project_ids, but SnubaParams in the added tests is constructed with `projects=` and does not provide `project_ids`; this will raise AttributeError at runtime when organization_events_stats calls this helper.

src/sentry/testutils/factories.py:_set_sample_rate_from_error_sampling uses `if client_sample_rate:`; a valid client_sample_rate of 0/0.0/'0' will be treated as falsy and sample_rate will not be set, causing incorrect sampling metadata/behavior for events with an explicit 0 rate.

src/sentry/api/helpers/error_upsampling.py:transform_query_columns_for_error_upsampling rewrites `count()` to `upsampled_count() as count`; forcing alias `count` can collide with existing aliases/expressions when callers already alias count or have multiple yAxes, leading to Snuba query errors or ambiguous/duplicate column resolution.

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/services/authz/rbac/service_test.go: TestService_CacheCheck uses a non-existent field in the request literal: it sets `Folder: "fold1"`, but the service code reads `checkReq.ParentFolder` (and the test’s denial key is built with parent "fold1"). This is a compile-time error if `CheckRequest` has no `Folder` field, or a logic bug if it does (the denial cache key will never match because `ParentFolder` remains empty).

pkg/services/authz/rbac/service_test.go: In TestService_CacheCheck/"Should deny on explicit cache deny entry", the test comment says it "Allow access ... to prove this is not checked" but it sets `permCache` to `{"dashboards:uid:dash1": false}` (deny). This is a concrete test bug/mismatch: it does not actually prove that the permission cache is ignored when a denial entry exists.
phase4_final: pkg/services/authz/rbac/service_test.go: TestService_CacheCheck uses a non-existent field in the request literal: it sets `Folder: "fold1"`, but the service code reads `checkReq.ParentFolder` (and the test’s denial key is built with parent "fold1"). This is a compile-time error if `CheckRequest` has no `Folder` field, or a logic bug if it does (the denial cache key will never match because `ParentFolder` remains empty).

pkg/services/authz/rbac/service_test.go: In TestService_CacheCheck/"Should deny on explicit cache deny entry", the test comment says it "Allow access ... to prove this is not checked" but it sets `permCache` to `{"dashboards:uid:dash1": false}` (deny). This is a concrete test bug/mismatch: it does not actually prove that the permission cache is ignored when a denial entry exists.

phase4_verified_raw: src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: OAuthLoginView.dispatch uses `state = pipeline.signature` as the OAuth `state` value. This is not a per-request nonce and is reused for the pipeline, weakening CSRF protection and enabling replay within the same pipeline signature.

src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: GitHubInstallation.dispatch compares `pipeline.fetch_state("github_authenticated_user") != integration.metadata["sender"]["login"]` without guarding for missing `sender`/`login` keys. If `integration.metadata` lacks these keys, this raises KeyError and 500s the install flow instead of rendering the error page.

src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: GitHubInstallation.dispatch now hard-depends on prior OAuthLoginView state `github_authenticated_user`. If that state is missing (e.g., pipeline state lost/partial), `pipeline.fetch_state(...)` returns None and the check will fail, causing an invalid-installation error and blocking otherwise valid installs (new wrong behavior introduced by this change).
phase4_final: src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: OAuthLoginView.dispatch uses `state = pipeline.signature` as the OAuth `state` value. This is not a per-request nonce and is reused for the pipeline, weakening CSRF protection and enabling replay within the same pipeline signature.

src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: GitHubInstallation.dispatch compares `pipeline.fetch_state("github_authenticated_user") != integration.metadata["sender"]["login"]` without guarding for missing `sender`/`login` keys. If `integration.metadata` lacks these keys, this raises KeyError and 500s the install flow instead of rendering the error page.

src/sentry/integrations/github/integration.py: GitHubInstallation.dispatch now hard-depends on prior OAuthLoginView state `github_authenticated_user`. If that state is missing (e.g., pipeline state lost/partial), `pipeline.fetch_state(...)` returns None and the check will fail, causing an invalid-installation error and blocking otherwise valid installs (new wrong behavior introduced by this change).

It starts with Objects::nonNull but then chains `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))`; Predicate.and evaluates the second predicate even when the first returns false, so calling getLoginPredicate().test(null) will throw NullPointerException (idp.getOrganizationId()).

model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/idp/InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.java: remove(String alias) can throw NullPointerException when alias does not exist. It does `IdentityProviderModel storedIdp = idpDelegate.getByAlias(alias);` (may be null) and then unconditionally calls `registerIDPLoginInvalidation(storedIdp)`, which calls `getLoginPredicate().test(idp)` and will NPE due to the non-null-safe predicate.

model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/idp/InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.java: update(IdentityProviderModel model) can throw NullPointerException if getById(model.getInternalId()) returns null (non-existent/deleted concurrently). It passes that null into registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(idpById, model), which calls getLoginPredicate().test(original) and will NPE due to the non-null-safe predicate.

model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/idp/InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.java: registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(original, updated) evaluates login predicate on the incoming 'updated' model rather than the persisted post-update state. If the update model is partial (e.g., missing config keys/flags), getLoginPredicate().test(updated) can differ from the actual stored state, causing missed invalidation (stale login caches) or unnecessary invalidation.
phase4_final: server-spi/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/IdentityProviderStorageProvider.java: LoginFilter.getLoginPredicate() is not null-safe. It starts with Objects::nonNull but then chains `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))`; Predicate.and evaluates the second predicate even when the first returns false, so calling getLoginPredicate().test(null) will throw NullPointerException (idp.getOrganizationId()).

model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/cache/infinispan/idp/InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.java: remove(String alias) can throw NullPointerException when alias does not exist. It does `IdentityProviderModel storedIdp = idpDelegate.getByAlias(alias);` (may be null) and then unconditionally calls `registerIDPLoginInvalidation(storedIdp)`, which calls `getLoginPredicate().test(idp)` and will NPE due to the non-null-safe predicate.

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/expr/reader.go: enableSqlExpressions() is implemented as a constant-false function (sets enabled := !h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...) and then returns false in all paths). As a result, ExpressionQueryReader.ReadQuery always rejects QueryTypeSQL with error "sqlExpressions is not implemented", even when the feature flag is enabled, breaking SQL query parsing/execution.

pkg/expr/sql/db.go: NewInMemoryDB() now returns a *DB whose core methods TablesList, RunCommands, and QueryFramesInto all return "not implemented" errors. This guarantees runtime failures for any SQL expression usage that relies on these methods.

pkg/expr/sql/parser.go: TablesList() was switched from duck.NewInMemoryDB() to NewInMemoryDB() (the new stub DB). It calls duckDB.RunCommands(...), which now always returns "not implemented", so TablesList will always fail for any input, breaking SQL table extraction.

pkg/expr/sql_command.go: (*SQLCommand).Execute() was switched from duck.NewInMemoryDB() to sql.NewInMemoryDB() (the new stub DB). It calls db.QueryFramesInto(...), which now always returns "not implemented", so every SQL expression execution will fail and set rsp.Error.
phase4_final: pkg/expr/reader.go: enableSqlExpressions() is implemented as a constant-false function (sets enabled := !h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...) and then returns false in all paths). As a result, ExpressionQueryReader.ReadQuery always rejects QueryTypeSQL with error "sqlExpressions is not implemented", even when the feature flag is enabled, breaking SQL query parsing/execution.

pkg/expr/sql/db.go: NewInMemoryDB() now returns a *DB whose core methods TablesList, RunCommands, and QueryFramesInto all return "not implemented" errors. This guarantees runtime failures for any SQL expression usage that relies on these methods.

pkg/expr/sql/parser.go: TablesList() was switched from duck.NewInMemoryDB() to NewInMemoryDB() (the new stub DB). It calls duckDB.RunCommands(...), which now always returns "not implemented", so TablesList will always fail for any input, breaking SQL table extraction.

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/api/webassets/webassets.go:GetWebAssets: TOCTOU/race in cache population: after RLock/RUnlock reads entryPointAssetsCache into ret, the function takes the write lock and proceeds without re-checking whether entryPointAssetsCache was populated by another goroutine in the interim. Under concurrent calls (non-Dev), multiple goroutines can redundantly recompute assets (CDN/file I/O) and the last writer can overwrite the cache, potentially returning inconsistent assets across callers.
phase4_final: pkg/api/webassets/webassets.go:GetWebAssets: TOCTOU/race in cache population: after RLock/RUnlock reads entryPointAssetsCache into ret, the function takes the write lock and proceeds without re-checking whether entryPointAssetsCache was populated by another goroutine in the interim. Under concurrent calls (non-Dev), multiple goroutines can redundantly recompute assets (CDN/file I/O) and the last writer can overwrite the cache, potentially returning inconsistent assets across callers.

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/cleanup_test.go:createTestAnnotations: Explicitly sets a.ID = int64(i+1) before inserting. If the annotation table uses auto-increment/identity IDs (common), forcing IDs can cause primary key conflicts or non-deterministic failures across DBs/runs, breaking integration tests.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:fetchIDs: Uses session.SQL(sql).Find(&ids) where ids is []int64. In xorm, Find typically expects a slice of structs; scanning a single column into []int64 via Find is not supported/works incorrectly, so this can error at runtime or return empty IDs, causing cleanup to delete nothing (wrong behavior) or fail.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:deleteByIDs: SQLite parameter-limit guard is wrong. It checks r.cfg.AnnotationCleanupJobBatchSize > 999, but the actual number of SQL parameters is len(ids). If batch size <= 999 but len(ids) > 999, it will still use placeholders and exceed SQLite's variable limit, causing runtime SQL errors.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:fetchIDs/deleteByIDs: SQL injection / unsafe SQL construction. Both functions interpolate `table` directly into SQL via fmt.Sprintf without validation/allowlist, and fetchIDs also interpolates `condition` directly into WHERE. This is a concrete injection sink if any caller can influence these strings, allowing arbitrary SQL execution.
phase4_final: pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/cleanup_test.go:createTestAnnotations: Explicitly sets a.ID = int64(i+1) before inserting. If the annotation table uses auto-increment/identity IDs (common), forcing IDs can cause primary key conflicts or non-deterministic failures across DBs/runs, breaking integration tests.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:fetchIDs: Uses session.SQL(sql).Find(&ids) where ids is []int64. In xorm, Find typically expects a slice of structs; scanning a single column into []int64 via Find is not supported/works incorrectly, so this can error at runtime or return empty IDs, causing cleanup to delete nothing (wrong behavior) or fail.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:deleteByIDs: SQLite parameter-limit guard is wrong. It checks r.cfg.AnnotationCleanupJobBatchSize > 999, but the actual number of SQL parameters is len(ids). If batch size <= 999 but len(ids) > 999, it will still use placeholders and exceed SQLite's variable limit, causing runtime SQL errors.

pkg/services/annotations/annotationsimpl/xorm_store.go:fetchIDs/deleteByIDs: SQL injection / unsafe SQL construction. Both functions interpolate `table` directly into SQL via fmt.Sprintf without validation/allowlist, and fetchIDs also interpolates `condition` directly into WHERE. This is a concrete injection sink if any caller can influence these strings, allowing arbitrary SQL execution.

pkg/apiserver/rest/dualwriter_mode3.go: DualWriterMode3.Delete records storage success duration with `d.recordStorageDuration(false, mode3Str, name, method, startStorage)` passing `name` where other calls use `options.Kind`; this corrupts the metric label value (kind label becomes object name).

phase4_verified_raw: GrafanaRuleListItem (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaRuleListItem.tsx): uses `const { name, uid, labels, provenance } = rule;` and passes `name` as the list item display name, but the previous implementation used `rulerRule.grafana_alert.title` as the user-facing name. This is a concrete behavior change visible in the UI (rule rows will show `promRule.name` instead of the title).

RuleActionsButtons early-null behavior (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/components/RuleActionsButtons.V2.tsx): `const identifier = getEditableIdentifier(...); if (!identifier) { return null; }` removes the entire actions UI (Edit + More menu) whenever an identifier cannot be constructed. This is a concrete regression vs prior behavior where actions could still render (e.g., at least the menu) even if edit wasn’t possible.

RuleActionsButtons prop type mismatch at call site (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaRuleListItem.tsx -> RuleActionsButtons.V2.tsx): GrafanaRuleListItem calls `<RuleActionsButtons promRule={rule} ... />` where `rule` is `GrafanaPromRuleDTO`, but RuleActionsButtons props declare `promRule?: Rule` (from app/types/unified-alerting). This is an incompatible type/shape; it can break runtime logic that expects a unified-alerting `Rule` (e.g., provisioned checks / type guards), and it also makes `getEditableIdentifier()` dependent on `prometheusRuleType.grafana.rule(promRule)` succeeding on a value that is not typed as a Prom DTO.

GrafanaGroupLoader removed reconciliation logic (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaGroupLoader.tsx): the diff deletes the Ruler group fetch and the matchRules-based rendering of Creating/Deleting transitional items (and deletes RuleOperationListItem rendering). This is a concrete functional regression: rules that exist only in Ruler (creating) or only in Prometheus (deleting) will no longer be shown with their operation state; they will simply be absent from the list.
phase4_final: GrafanaRuleListItem (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaRuleListItem.tsx): uses `const { name, uid, labels, provenance } = rule;` and passes `name` as the list item display name, but the previous implementation used `rulerRule.grafana_alert.title` as the user-facing name. This is a concrete behavior change visible in the UI (rule rows will show `promRule.name` instead of the title).

RuleActionsButtons early-null behavior (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/components/RuleActionsButtons.V2.tsx): `const identifier = getEditableIdentifier(...); if (!identifier) { return null; }` removes the entire actions UI (Edit + More menu) whenever an identifier cannot be constructed. This is a concrete regression vs prior behavior where actions could still render (e.g., at least the menu) even if edit wasn’t possible.

RuleActionsButtons prop type mismatch at call site (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaRuleListItem.tsx -> RuleActionsButtons.V2.tsx): GrafanaRuleListItem calls `<RuleActionsButtons promRule={rule} ... />` where `rule` is `GrafanaPromRuleDTO`, but RuleActionsButtons props declare `promRule?: Rule` (from app/types/unified-alerting). This is an incompatible type/shape; it can break runtime logic that expects a unified-alerting `Rule` (e.g., provisioned checks / type guards), and it also makes `getEditableIdentifier()` dependent on `prometheusRuleType.grafana.rule(promRule)` succeeding on a value that is not typed as a Prom DTO.

GrafanaGroupLoader removed reconciliation logic (public/app/features/alerting/unified/rule-list/GrafanaGroupLoader.tsx): the diff deletes the Ruler group fetch and the matchRules-based rendering of Creating/Deleting transitional items (and deletes RuleOperationListItem rendering). This is a concrete functional regression: rules that exist only in Ruler (creating) or only in Prometheus (deleting) will no longer be shown with their operation state; they will simply be absent from the list.

phase4_verified_raw: public/app/plugins/datasource/loki/querySplitting.ts: runSplitQuery now maps request.targets through datasource.applyTemplateVariables(query, ...) using the original query objects. The updated tests mock applyTemplateVariables as mutating its input (e.g., query.expr = ...), so this change introduces a confirmed in-place mutation of request.targets during query selection/splitting, which can corrupt subsequent uses of the same request object (retries/re-execution/middleware expecting un-interpolated targets).

public/app/plugins/datasource/loki/shardQuerySplitting.ts: runShardSplitQuery now maps request.targets through datasource.applyTemplateVariables(query, ...) on the original query objects. The updated tests mock applyTemplateVariables as mutating its input, so this change introduces a confirmed in-place mutation of request.targets, which can break later logic if the same request object is reused.
phase4_final: public/app/plugins/datasource/loki/querySplitting.ts: runSplitQuery now maps request.targets through datasource.applyTemplateVariables(query, ...) using the original query objects. The updated tests mock applyTemplateVariables as mutating its input (e.g., query.expr = ...), so this change introduces a confirmed in-place mutation of request.targets during query selection/splitting, which can corrupt subsequent uses of the same request object (retries/re-execution/middleware expecting un-interpolated targets).

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go: AnonDBStore.updateDevice() returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected==0. This is wrong behavior: rowsAffected==0 can mean the device_id does not exist or is outside the updated_at window, not that the device limit was reached. Result: when the limit is reached, attempts to update an existing device can incorrectly fail with ErrDeviceLimitReached, blocking legitimate updates.

pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/client.go: Anonymous.Authenticate() now calls TagDevice synchronously on the request ctx and returns an error when ErrDeviceLimitReached. This is a concrete behavior change from the previous async/background tagging: authentication can now fail solely because the anonymous device limit is reached (and also becomes sensitive to request cancellation/deadlines), which is a functional regression compared to the prior code path that never blocked auth on tagging.

pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go: CreateOrUpdateDevice() enforces the device limit via CountDevices(...) then insert/update, which is a TOCTOU race. Concurrent requests can both observe count < limit and both insert, exceeding the configured limit under load (limit not actually enforced).
phase4_final: pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go: AnonDBStore.updateDevice() returns ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected==0. Result: when the limit is reached, attempts to update an existing device can incorrectly fail with ErrDeviceLimitReached, blocking legitimate updates.

phase4_verified_raw: app/controllers/application_controller.rb#set_locale: Unconditionally calls `I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!`. If `I18n.fallbacks` is nil or does not respond to `ensure_loaded!` (e.g., initializer not run in a given boot path), this will raise `NoMethodError` and can break requests hitting `set_locale`.

config/initializers/i18n.rb#FallbackLocaleList#[]: Returns `[locale, SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym, :en]` without normalizing `locale` to a Symbol. If `locale` is a String, the list can contain both "en" and `:en`, defeating `.uniq` and causing duplicate/incorrect locale loading and fallback behavior.

config/initializers/i18n.rb#FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded!: Calls `I18n.ensure_loaded! l`, but the diff adds `ensure_loaded!(locale)` to `lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb` (a backend method), not to the `I18n` module. Unless `I18n.ensure_loaded!` already exists elsewhere, this will raise `NoMethodError: undefined method 'ensure_loaded!' for I18n:Module` when `ensure_loaded!` runs.
phase4_final: app/controllers/application_controller.rb#set_locale: Unconditionally calls `I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!`. If `I18n.fallbacks` is nil or does not respond to `ensure_loaded!` (e.g., initializer not run in a given boot path), this will raise `NoMethodError` and can break requests hitting `set_locale`.

config/initializers/i18n.rb#FallbackLocaleList#[]: Returns `[locale, SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym, :en]` without normalizing `locale` to a Symbol. If `locale` is a String, the list can contain both "en" and `:en`, defeating `.uniq` and causing duplicate/incorrect locale loading and fallback behavior.

config/initializers/i18n.rb#FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded!: Calls `I18n.ensure_loaded! l`, but the diff adds `ensure_loaded!(locale)` to `lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb` (a backend method), not to the `I18n` module. Unless `I18n.ensure_loaded!` already exists elsewhere, this will raise `NoMethodError: undefined method 'ensure_loaded!' for I18n:Module` when `ensure_loaded!` runs.

phase4_verified_raw: pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/metrics_middleware.go: NewMetricsMiddleware returns a middleware that reuses a single *MetricsMiddleware instance (imw) and mutates imw.next on each decoration (imw.next = next). Decorating multiple clients with the same middleware will overwrite next, causing requests to be routed to the wrong client and creating a data race under concurrent use.

pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go: CreateMiddlewares changes middleware order so NewContextualLoggerMiddleware runs AFTER NewMetricsMiddleware. As a result, MetricsMiddleware will not see the contextual logger attributes in ctx (endpoint/pluginId/ds/user) that are added by ContextualLoggerMiddleware, so any metrics/logging inside MetricsMiddleware that relies on those ctx attributes will be missing them (behavior regression vs. previous InstrumentationMiddleware which enriched ctx itself).
phase4_final: pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/metrics_middleware.go: NewMetricsMiddleware returns a middleware that reuses a single *MetricsMiddleware instance (imw) and mutates imw.next on each decoration (imw.next = next). Decorating multiple clients with the same middleware will overwrite next, causing requests to be routed to the wrong client and creating a data race under concurrent use.

pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go: CreateMiddlewares changes middleware order so NewContextualLoggerMiddleware runs AFTER NewMetricsMiddleware. As a result, MetricsMiddleware will not see the contextual logger attributes in ctx (endpoint/pluginId/ds/user) that are added by ContextualLoggerMiddleware, so any metrics/logging inside MetricsMiddleware that relies on those ctx attributes will be missing them (behavior regression vs. previous InstrumentationMiddleware which enriched ctx itself).

db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb: builds SQL via string interpolation `VALUES ('#{h}', ...)` using existing `site_settings.embeddable_hosts` lines; a host containing a single quote will break the migration (and is SQL-injection-prone in general). Use bound params / `quote` / `insert` helpers.

app/models/embeddable_host.rb#before_validation: calls `self.host.sub!` unconditionally; if `host` is nil (e.g., new record or missing param), this raises `NoMethodError` during validation instead of producing a validation error.

app/models/embeddable_host.rb#record_for_host: compares `where("lower(host) = ?", host)` but does not downcase the RHS; for mixed-case input URLs (e.g. `http://EvilTrout.com`), lookup fails even if the stored host is `eviltrout.com`, incorrectly rejecting allowed hosts.

app/models/topic_embed.rb#import: sets `category: eh.try(:category_id)`; when no matching EmbeddableHost exists, this passes nil and changes behavior from the previous `SiteSetting.embed_category` fallback, potentially creating embedded topics in the default category (or failing depending on PostCreator/category requirements).
phase4_final: app/controllers/admin/embeddable_hosts_controller.rb#update: `host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first` can return nil; `save_host(host)` then calls `host.host = ...` and crashes with `NoMethodError`, returning 500 instead of a 404/validation error.

db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb: builds SQL via string interpolation `VALUES ('#{h}', ...)` using existing `site_settings.embeddable_hosts` lines; a host containing a single quote will break the migration (and is SQL-injection-prone in general). Use bound params / `quote` / `insert` helpers.

app/models/embeddable_host.rb#before_validation: calls `self.host.sub!` unconditionally; if `host` is nil (e.g., new record or missing param), this raises `NoMethodError` during validation instead of producing a validation error.

app/models/embeddable_host.rb#record_for_host: compares `where("lower(host) = ?", host)` but does not downcase the RHS; for mixed-case input URLs (e.g. `http://EvilTrout.com`), lookup fails even if the stored host is `eviltrout.com`, incorrectly rejecting allowed hosts.

app/models/topic_embed.rb#import: sets `category: eh.try(:category_id)`; when no matching EmbeddableHost exists, this passes nil and changes behavior from the previous `SiteSetting.embed_category` fallback, potentially creating embedded topics in the default category (or failing depending on PostCreator/category requirements).
