phase4_verified_raw: app/assets/javascripts/discourse/models/group.js: findMembers() returns `undefined` when `name` is empty (`if (Em.isEmpty(this.get('name'))) { return ; }`). Callers (e.g. admin_group_route.js and group-members route) call `model.findMembers()` and may rely on a thenable; this breaks the previous contract (was `Ember.RSVP.resolve([])`) and can cause runtime errors if chained or leave state unset.

app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/group/members.hbs: template changed from `{{#each m in model}}` to `{{#each m in members}}`, but discourse/routes/group-members.js.es6 now sets `controller.set("model", model)` and does not set a `members` property on the controller. Result: `members` is undefined and the members list will not render.

app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group.hbs: uses `{{each member in members itemView="group-member"}}` but admin/routes/admin_group_route.js only sets `controller.set("model", model)` and does not set `members` on the controller. Since Group.findMembers now sets `members` on the model, the template should reference `model.members` (or controller must proxy/set `members`); as written the list can render empty/undefined.

app/assets/javascripts/admin/views/group-member.js.es6: `templateName: "admin/templates/group_member"` does not match the template file path `app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group_member.hbs` which will be registered as `admin/group_member` (no `templates/` segment). This will cause missing-template errors / blank rendering for the group-member view.

spec/controllers/admin/groups_controller_spec.rb: in context ".remove_member" the test calls `xhr :put, :remove_member, ...` but routes/controller define remove_member as a DELETE endpoint (`delete "members" => "groups#remove_member"` and controller method is `remove_member`). Using PUT here is a method mismatch and will fail to exercise the intended route/behavior.

app/controllers/admin/groups_controller.rb: remove_member uses `group.users.delete(user_id)` passing an integer id. This is not equivalent to removing the membership in all association configurations and can delete the wrong thing or fail to remove the join row depending on association type; correct removal should target the user record or the join model explicitly. As written it can silently not remove the membership.

app/assets/javascripts/admin/controllers/admin-group.js.es6: save action no longer sends usernames when creating/updating a group (changed from `saveWithUsernames(this.get('usernames'))` / `createWithUsernames(...)` to `group.save()` / `group.create()`). Since the UI still collects `usernames` and the previous behavior added members on save/create, this is a functional regression: initial members entered in the selector will be ignored unless the separate addMembers action is used.
phase4_final: app/assets/javascripts/discourse/models/group.js: findMembers() returns `undefined` when `name` is empty (`if (Em.isEmpty(this.get('name'))) { return ; }`). Callers (e.g. admin_group_route.js and group-members route) call `model.findMembers()` and may rely on a thenable; this breaks the previous contract (was `Ember.RSVP.resolve([])`) and can cause runtime errors if chained or leave state unset.

app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/group/members.hbs: template changed from `{{#each m in model}}` to `{{#each m in members}}`, but discourse/routes/group-members.js.es6 now sets `controller.set("model", model)` and does not set a `members` property on the controller. Result: `members` is undefined and the members list will not render.

app/assets/javascripts/admin/views/group-member.js.es6: `templateName: "admin/templates/group_member"` does not match the template file path `app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group_member.hbs` which will be registered as `admin/group_member` (no `templates/` segment). This will cause missing-template errors / blank rendering for the group-member view.

spec/controllers/admin/groups_controller_spec.rb: in context ".remove_member" the test calls `xhr :put, :remove_member, ...` but routes/controller define remove_member as a DELETE endpoint (`delete "members" => "groups#remove_member"` and controller method is `remove_member`). Using PUT here is a method mismatch and will fail to exercise the intended route/behavior.

app/controllers/admin/groups_controller.rb: remove_member uses `group.users.delete(user_id)` passing an integer id. This is not equivalent to removing the membership in all association configurations and can delete the wrong thing or fail to remove the join row depending on association type; correct removal should target the user record or the join model explicitly. As written it can silently not remove the membership.

app/assets/javascripts/admin/controllers/admin-group.js.es6: save action no longer sends usernames when creating/updating a group (changed from `saveWithUsernames(this.get('usernames'))` / `createWithUsernames(...)` to `group.save()` / `group.create()`). Since the UI still collects `usernames` and the previous behavior added members on save/create, this is a functional regression: initial members entered in the selector will be ignored unless the separate addMembers action is used.

phase4_verified_raw: lib/validators/email_validator.rb: email_in_restriction_setting? builds an unanchored regex `Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` from the whitelist/blacklist setting. This will match substrings and can be bypassed / cause false positives (e.g., allowing `gmail.com` also matches `user@gmail.com.evil.com`), leading to incorrect allow/deny behavior.

app/models/blocked_email.rb: BlockedEmail.should_block?(email) has hidden write side effects (increments match_count, sets last_match_at, saves) even though it’s a predicate. Since it’s called from EmailValidator during validation, merely validating an email mutates the DB and changes behavior across repeated validations.

app/models/blocked_email.rb: BlockedEmail.should_block?(email) updates match_count via non-atomic read-modify-write (`record.match_count += 1; record.save`). Concurrent validations can lose increments / produce inconsistent last_match_at (race condition / lost update).
phase4_final: lib/validators/email_validator.rb: email_in_restriction_setting? builds an unanchored regex `Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` from the whitelist/blacklist setting. phase4_verified_raw: app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss: @mixin align-items adds invalid vendor property `-ms-align-items`. IE10/11 do not support `-ms-align-items` (they use `-ms-flex-align`), so this declaration is ignored and can lead to missing/incorrect vertical alignment in IE10/11 for new `@include align-items(...)` usages (e.g., header.scss, badges.css.scss, topic-post.scss).

app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss: @mixin order uses `-webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group: $val` without converting from modern 0-based `order` to legacy 1-based ordinal-group. This causes incorrect ordering in legacy WebKit/old Firefox box implementations (e.g., `@include order(3)` will not match `order: 3`; it should be `$val + 1`).

app/assets/stylesheets/common/base/topic.scss: `.extra-info-wrapper` now includes `@include order(2);` but the diff does not show its parent being a flex container in this scope. If the parent is not `display:flex`, `order` has no effect, so the intended reordering will not occur (behavioral bug).
phase4_final: app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss: @mixin align-items adds invalid vendor property `-ms-align-items`. IE10/11 do not support `-ms-align-items` (they use `-ms-flex-align`), so this declaration is ignored and can lead to missing/incorrect vertical alignment in IE10/11 for new `@include align-items(...)` usages (e.g., header.scss, badges.css.scss, topic-post.scss).

app/assets/stylesheets/common/foundation/mixins.scss: @mixin order uses `-webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group: $val` without converting from modern 0-based `order` to legacy 1-based ordinal-group. This causes incorrect ordering in legacy WebKit/old Firefox box implementations (e.g., `@include order(3)` will not match `order: 3`; it should be `$val + 1`).

app/assets/stylesheets/common/base/topic.scss: `.extra-info-wrapper` now includes `@include order(2);` but the diff does not show its parent being a flex container in this scope. If the parent is not `display:flex`, `order` has no effect, so the intended reordering will not occur (behavioral bug).

phase4_verified_raw: app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: UserSerializer defines `def include_website_name` but ActiveModel::Serializer conditional attribute hook requires `include_website_name?`. As written, the predicate will not be called, so `website_name` will be serialized unconditionally (behavior change; may emit `website_name: null` when website is blank).

app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: `website_name` compares hosts case-sensitively (`website_host == discourse_host`, `ends_with?`), but DNS hostnames are case-insensitive. Mixed-case `Discourse.current_hostname` or stored website host will take the wrong branch and return the wrong display value (host-only vs host+path).

app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: `website_name` uses `discourse_host.ends_with?('.' << website_host)` with raw `website_host`, which can include a port (e.g., `example.com:3000`). In that case the parent-domain match will fail and it will incorrectly return only `website_host` (no path) even when the website domain is the parent of the instance domain.
phase4_final: app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: UserSerializer defines `def include_website_name` but ActiveModel::Serializer conditional attribute hook requires `include_website_name?`. As written, the predicate will not be called, so `website_name` will be serialized unconditionally (behavior change; may emit `website_name: null` when website is blank).

app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: `website_name` compares hosts case-sensitively (`website_host == discourse_host`, `ends_with?`), but DNS hostnames are case-insensitive. Mixed-case `Discourse.current_hostname` or stored website host will take the wrong branch and return the wrong display value (host-only vs host+path).

app/serializers/user_serializer.rb: `website_name` uses `discourse_host.ends_with?('.' << website_host)` with raw `website_host`, which can include a port (e.g., `example.com:3000`). In that case the parent-domain match will fail and it will incorrectly return only `website_host` (no path) even when the website domain is the parent of the instance domain.

phase4_verified_raw: lib/topic_retriever.rb: TopicRetriever#invalid_host? calls `URI(@embed_url).host` without requiring 'uri'; in Ruby this can raise `NameError: uninitialized constant TopicRetriever::URI` depending on load order, breaking retrieval for embeds.

app/controllers/embed_controller.rb: EmbedController#ensure_embeddable compares `URI(request.referer || '').host != SiteSetting.embeddable_host` case-sensitively; hostnames are case-insensitive so valid referers with different casing will be rejected, breaking embedding.

lib/topic_retriever.rb: TopicRetriever#invalid_host? compares `SiteSetting.embeddable_host != URI(@embed_url).host` case-sensitively; hostnames are case-insensitive so valid embed URLs with different casing will be rejected, preventing retrieval.

app/assets/javascripts/embed.js: postMessageReceived origin validation is incorrect: `if (discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1) return;` treats `discourseUrl` as a haystack and `e.origin` as a substring. This can accept unintended origins if `e.origin` is a substring of `discourseUrl`, and can reject legitimate messages when formatting differs (path/trailing slash/port), breaking resize validation.

app/views/layouts/embed.html.erb: postMessage targetOrigin is set to `<%= request.referer %>` (a full URL including path/query) instead of an origin (scheme+host+port). Browsers require targetOrigin to be a valid origin or '*', so this can throw/ignore the message and break iframe resizing.

app/models/topic_embed.rb: TopicEmbed.import has a TOCTOU race: it does `embed = TopicEmbed.where(embed_url: url).first` then later `TopicEmbed.create!` when blank. Concurrent imports of the same URL can both see blank and then one will raise `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique`, intermittently failing imports.
phase4_final: lib/topic_retriever.rb: TopicRetriever#invalid_host? calls `URI(@embed_url).host` without requiring 'uri'; in Ruby this can raise `NameError: uninitialized constant TopicRetriever::URI` depending on load order, breaking retrieval for embeds.

app/controllers/embed_controller.rb: EmbedController#ensure_embeddable compares `URI(request.referer || '').host != SiteSetting.embeddable_host` case-sensitively; hostnames are case-insensitive so valid referers with different casing will be rejected, breaking embedding.

app/assets/javascripts/embed.js: postMessageReceived origin validation is incorrect: `if (discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1) return;` treats `discourseUrl` as a haystack and `e.origin` as a substring. This can accept unintended origins if `e.origin` is a substring of `discourseUrl`, and can reject legitimate messages when formatting differs (path/trailing slash/port), breaking resize validation.

app/views/layouts/embed.html.erb: postMessage targetOrigin is set to `<%= request.referer %>` (a full URL including path/query) instead of an origin (scheme+host+port). Browsers require targetOrigin to be a valid origin or '*', so this can throw/ignore the message and break iframe resizing.

app/models/topic_embed.rb: TopicEmbed.import has a TOCTOU race: it does `embed = TopicEmbed.where(embed_url: url).first` then later `TopicEmbed.create!` when blank. Concurrent imports of the same URL can both see blank and then one will raise `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique`, intermittently failing imports.

phase4_verified_raw: app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb#create_upload: In the API URL branch (file.nil? && url.present? && is_api?), content_type is never assigned, but Upload.create_for(..., content_type: content_type, ...) is still called. app/assets/javascripts/discourse/lib/utilities.js: Client-side max upload size validation was changed from Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'] to a hardcoded 10 * 1024 (10MB). This will incorrectly reject uploads when the server allows >10MB and allow attempts when the server limit is <10MB, causing inconsistent/wrong user-visible behavior.

app/assets/javascripts/discourse/lib/utilities.js: The 413 (entity too large) handler was changed from Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb to a hardcoded 10 * 1024 (10MB), so the error message will report the wrong limit when the site setting differs, misleading users and making UI/server validation inconsistent.

app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb#create_upload: If FileHelper.download(...) fails and returns nil, the new downsizing guard checks `tempfile && ...`, but the code still unconditionally calls Upload.create_for(..., tempfile, filename, tempfile.size, ...). This will crash with NoMethodError on `nil.size` (and likely other nil dereferences) for failed downloads.

app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb#create_upload: The API URL download limit was changed from SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes to a fixed 10.megabytes. This breaks the server-side contract: URLs larger than 10MB will be rejected even if the site setting allows more, and URLs up to 10MB may be downloaded even if the site setting is lower, leading to incorrect behavior relative to configuration.
phase4_final: app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb#create_upload: In the API URL branch (file.nil? && url.present? && is_api?), content_type is never assigned, but Upload.create_for(..., content_type: content_type, ...) is still called. This will raise NameError: undefined local variable or method `content_type` at runtime for API URL uploads.

This will crash with NoMethodError on `nil.size` (and likely other nil dereferences) for failed downloads.

app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb#create_upload: The API URL download limit was changed from SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes to a fixed 10.megabytes. This breaks the server-side contract: URLs larger than 10MB will be rejected even if the site setting allows more, and URLs up to 10MB may be downloaded even if the site setting is lower, leading to incorrect behavior relative to configuration.

phase4_verified_raw: app/models/topic_user.rb: TopicUser.track_visit!(topic,user) sets `user_id = user.is_a?(User) ? user.id : topic` — else branch uses `topic` instead of the passed `user` (likely an integer user_id). This will update/create the wrong topic_users row (user_id becomes a Topic object/id).

app/controllers/topics_controller.rb: TopicsController#unsubscribe does `tu = TopicUser.find_by(...)` and then immediately calls `tu.notification_level` without a nil check. If no TopicUser row exists for that user/topic, this raises NoMethodError and the action 500s.

app/controllers/topics_controller.rb: perform_show_response format.html now explicitly `render :show` after `store_preloaded(...)`. This method is called from both show and unsubscribe; forcing an explicit render here can trigger ActionController::DoubleRenderError if any caller path already rendered/redirected before calling perform_show_response.

app/assets/javascripts/discourse/controllers/topic-unsubscribe.js.es6 + app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/topic/unsubscribe.hbs: computed property is named `stopNotificiationsText` (typo) and template references `{{{stopNotificiationsText}}}`. This typo becomes part of the public API; any intended `stopNotificationsText` usage will fail, and the misspelling is a real identifier bug.

app/mailers/user_notifications.rb: send_notification_email unconditionally sets `unsubscribe_url: post.topic.unsubscribe_url`. If `post.topic` is nil (e.g., deleted topic or not loaded in this code path), this will raise NoMethodError and prevent email delivery.
phase4_final: app/models/topic_user.rb: TopicUser.track_visit!(topic,user) sets `user_id = user.is_a?(User) ? user.id : topic` — else branch uses `topic` instead of the passed `user` (likely an integer user_id). This will update/create the wrong topic_users row (user_id becomes a Topic object/id).

app/controllers/topics_controller.rb: TopicsController#unsubscribe does `tu = TopicUser.find_by(...)` and then immediately calls `tu.notification_level` without a nil check. If no TopicUser row exists for that user/topic, this raises NoMethodError and the action 500s.

app/controllers/topics_controller.rb: perform_show_response format.html now explicitly `render :show` after `store_preloaded(...)`. This method is called from both show and unsubscribe; forcing an explicit render here can trigger ActionController::DoubleRenderError if any caller path already rendered/redirected before calling perform_show_response.

app/assets/javascripts/discourse/controllers/topic-unsubscribe.js.es6 + app/assets/javascripts/discourse/templates/topic/unsubscribe.hbs: computed property is named `stopNotificiationsText` (typo) and template references `{{{stopNotificiationsText}}}`. This typo becomes part of the public API; any intended `stopNotificationsText` usage will fail, and the misspelling is a real identifier bug.

app/mailers/user_notifications.rb: send_notification_email unconditionally sets `unsubscribe_url: post.topic.unsubscribe_url`. If `post.topic` is nil (e.g., deleted topic or not loaded in this code path), this will raise NoMethodError and prevent email delivery.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts::checkIfIsAvailable — Working-hours boundary check is broken: `end` is computed from `slotStartTime` instead of `slotEndTime` (`const end = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute();`). This will incorrectly treat slots that extend past `workingHour.endTime` as within working hours.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts::checkIfIsAvailable — Date override equality check is incorrect: `if (dayjs(date.start).add(utcOffset, "minutes") === dayjs(date.end).add(utcOffset, "minutes"))` compares Dayjs object references, so it will never be true for equal instants. This breaks the intended `start == end` override behavior.

packages/lib/slots.ts::getSlots — New override offset logic can crash or compute wrong offsets when `override.timeZone` is missing: `dayjs(override.start.toString()).tz(override.timeZone).utcOffset()` calls `tz()` with a possibly-undefined timezone (TimeRange.timeZone is optional in the diff), which can throw or yield incorrect offsets, breaking slot calculations for overrides without a timezone.

packages/lib/slots.ts::getSlots — Override offset math is incorrect because it reparses `override.start.toString()` (locale/runtime-timezone dependent) before applying `.tz(...)`. This can shift the instant and produce wrong `organizerUtcOffset/inviteeUtcOffset`, leading to incorrect override start/end minutes and wrong slot availability.
phase4_final: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts::checkIfIsAvailable — Working-hours boundary check is broken: `end` is computed from `slotStartTime` instead of `slotEndTime` (`const end = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute();`). This will incorrectly treat slots that extend past `workingHour.endTime` as within working hours.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts::checkIfIsAvailable — Date override equality check is incorrect: `if (dayjs(date.start).add(utcOffset, "minutes") === dayjs(date.end).add(utcOffset, "minutes"))` compares Dayjs object references, so it will never be true for equal instants. This breaks the intended `start == end` override behavior.

packages/lib/slots.ts::getSlots — New override offset logic can crash or compute wrong offsets when `override.timeZone` is missing: `dayjs(override.start.toString()).tz(override.timeZone).utcOffset()` calls `tz()` with a possibly-undefined timezone (TimeRange.timeZone is optional in the diff), which can throw or yield incorrect offsets, breaking slot calculations for overrides without a timezone.

packages/lib/slots.ts::getSlots — Override offset math is incorrect because it reparses `override.start.toString()` (locale/runtime-timezone dependent) before applying `.tz(...)`. This can shift the instant and produce wrong `organizerUtcOffset/inviteeUtcOffset`, leading to incorrect override start/end minutes and wrong slot availability.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleSMSReminders.ts: handler(): The deleteMany() where clause was changed to OR([ { method: SMS, scheduledDate <= now }, { retryCount > 1 } ]). The retryCount branch is not constrained by method or scheduledDate, so it will delete WorkflowReminder rows of any method (email/push/etc.) and any scheduledDate as soon as retryCount > 1, which is incorrect behavior introduced by this PR.

packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleSMSReminders.ts: handler(): retryCount is incremented via read-modify-write (data: { retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1 }) using the value read earlier from findMany(). Concurrent executions can race and overwrite each other, losing increments (non-atomic update), breaking retryCount tracking.

packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleSMSReminders.ts: handler(): In both the new else branch and the catch block, prisma.workflowReminder.update({ where: { id: reminder.id } ... }) assumes the row still exists. Because the handler now runs a deleteMany() that can delete rows with retryCount > 1 (and is not scoped), an update can throw a 'Record to update not found' error and fail the handler instead of skipping.
phase4_final: packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleSMSReminders.ts: handler(): The deleteMany() where clause was changed to OR([ { method: SMS, scheduledDate <= now }, { retryCount > 1 } ]). The retryCount branch is not constrained by method or scheduledDate, so it will delete WorkflowReminder rows of any method (email/push/etc.) and any scheduledDate as soon as retryCount > 1, which is incorrect behavior introduced by this PR.

Because the handler now runs a deleteMany() that can delete rows with retryCount > 1 (and is not scoped), an update can throw a 'Record to update not found' error and fail the handler instead of skipping.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildFilterConditions() uses truthiness checks `if (this.filters.eventTypeId)` and `if (this.filters.memberUserId)`. If either filter is `0`, the condition is skipped and the filter is not applied, returning broader results than intended.

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: getBaseConditions() branches on `if (authConditions && filterConditions)` etc., but `authConditions` is always a Prisma.Sql object (even for NOTHING_CONDITION). This makes the `else if (filterConditions)` and final `else` effectively unreachable and causes `getBaseConditions()` to return `(${authConditions}) AND (${filterConditions})` even when `filterConditions` is null, producing invalid SQL / runtime error when used in `$queryRaw`.

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildOrgAuthorizationCondition() and buildTeamAuthorizationCondition() interpolate plain JS arrays into `ANY(${teamIds})` / `ANY(${uniqueUserIds})` / `ANY(${userIdsFromTeam})`. In Prisma raw SQL this does not reliably produce a typed SQL array; it can yield invalid SQL or type mismatch at runtime unless explicitly constructed/cast (e.g., `ARRAY[...]::int[]`).

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildTeamAuthorizationCondition() changes behavior when `userIdsFromTeam` is empty: it omits the non-team-booking branch entirely. Previously the OR always included `{ userId: { in: [] }, isTeamBooking: false }` (always false), so the new behavior can broaden results by allowing team bookings without preserving the explicit false branch (behavioral change visible in diff).

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: constructor type changed to accept `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions` but the class property is still typed as `InsightsBookingServiceOptions | null`. This is a type/API contract mismatch in the class definition that can break type-checking and callers relying on the zod-inferred discriminated union type.
phase4_final: packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildFilterConditions() uses truthiness checks `if (this.filters.eventTypeId)` and `if (this.filters.memberUserId)`. If either filter is `0`, the condition is skipped and the filter is not applied, returning broader results than intended.

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: getBaseConditions() branches on `if (authConditions && filterConditions)` etc., but `authConditions` is always a Prisma.Sql object (even for NOTHING_CONDITION). This makes the `else if (filterConditions)` and final `else` effectively unreachable and causes `getBaseConditions()` to return `(${authConditions}) AND (${filterConditions})` even when `filterConditions` is null, producing invalid SQL / runtime error when used in `$queryRaw`.

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildOrgAuthorizationCondition() and buildTeamAuthorizationCondition() interpolate plain JS arrays into `ANY(${teamIds})` / `ANY(${uniqueUserIds})` / `ANY(${userIdsFromTeam})`. In Prisma raw SQL this does not reliably produce a typed SQL array; it can yield invalid SQL or type mismatch at runtime unless explicitly constructed/cast (e.g., `ARRAY[...]::int[]`).

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: buildTeamAuthorizationCondition() changes behavior when `userIdsFromTeam` is empty: it omits the non-team-booking branch entirely. Previously the OR always included `{ userId: { in: [] }, isTeamBooking: false }` (always false), so the new behavior can broaden results by allowing team bookings without preserving the explicit false branch (behavioral change visible in diff).

packages/lib/server/service/insightsBooking.ts: constructor type changed to accept `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions` but the class property is still typed as `InsightsBookingServiceOptions | null`. This is a type/API contract mismatch in the class definition that can break type-checking and callers relying on the zod-inferred discriminated union type.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts: minimumTokenResponseSchema uses computed property keys inside z.object() (`[z.string().toString()]` and `[z.string().optional().toString()]`), which is invalid/meaningless (evaluates to the literal key "[object Object]") and will not validate arbitrary expiry/extra fields as intended; this breaks token parsing/validation at runtime.

packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts + callers: when APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED branch is taken, refreshOAuthTokens returns a Fetch Response, but multiple callers treat the return as an Axios-style object with `.data` (e.g., googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts does `const token = res?.data;` and zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts does `if (!tokenInfo.data.error)`), causing undefined access and broken refresh/update logic.

packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts: credential-sync POST omits a Content-Type header while sending URLSearchParams; many servers require `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` to parse the body, so the sync endpoint may not receive calcomUserId/appSlug and refresh will fail.

packages/lib/constants.ts: APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED is exported as `process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET && process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, which yields a string (or empty string) rather than a boolean; any strict boolean checks or type expectations will break and the value is not reliably true/false.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: after refresh it calls `parseRefreshTokenResponse(googleCredentials, googleCredentialSchema)` (parsing the pre-existing credential object) instead of parsing the refreshed token payload (`token`/response data); this can validate stale data and write incorrect keys to Prisma or throw even when refresh succeeded.

packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts: refreshOAuthTokens is called with `credentialId` as the third argument, but refreshOAuthTokens expects a Cal.com userId (number|null) and sends it as `calcomUserId`; this will call the sync endpoint with the credential record id, refreshing the wrong user or failing refresh entirely.
phase4_final: packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts: minimumTokenResponseSchema uses computed property keys inside z.object() (`[z.string().toString()]` and `[z.string().optional().toString()]`), which is invalid/meaningless (evaluates to the literal key "[object Object]") and will not validate arbitrary expiry/extra fields as intended; this breaks token parsing/validation at runtime.

packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts + callers: when APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED branch is taken, refreshOAuthTokens returns a Fetch Response, but multiple callers treat the return as an Axios-style object with `.data` (e.g., googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts does `const token = res?.data;` and zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts does `if (!tokenInfo.data.error)`), causing undefined access and broken refresh/update logic.

packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts: credential-sync POST omits a Content-Type header while sending URLSearchParams; many servers require `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` to parse the body, so the sync endpoint may not receive calcomUserId/appSlug and refresh will fail.

packages/lib/constants.ts: APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED is exported as `process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET && process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, which yields a string (or empty string) rather than a boolean; any strict boolean checks or type expectations will break and the value is not reliably true/false.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: after refresh it calls `parseRefreshTokenResponse(googleCredentials, googleCredentialSchema)` (parsing the pre-existing credential object) instead of parsing the refreshed token payload (`token`/response data); this can validate stale data and write incorrect keys to Prisma or throw even when refresh succeeded.

packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts: refreshOAuthTokens is called with `credentialId` as the third argument, but refreshOAuthTokens expects a Cal.com userId (number|null) and sends it as `calcomUserId`; this will call the sync endpoint with the credential record id, refreshing the wrong user or failing refresh entirely.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts: handler(): uses booking.workflowReminders.forEach(...) to call async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder but never awaits the returned promises. The function then proceeds to await only attendeeDeletes/bookingReferenceDeletes/apiDeletes, so reminder cancellation/deletion can be dropped (wrong behavior, especially if the process/request ends early).

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx: bookingsRouter (reschedule path): bookingToReschedule.workflowReminders.forEach(...) calls async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting. The previous code awaited Promise.all(remindersToDelete); now nothing is awaited, so reminders may not be cancelled/deleted before the mutation continues/returns.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx: workflowsRouter: multiple reminder-cancellation loops call async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting (e.g., scheduledReminders.forEach, remindersToDelete.flat().forEach, remindersFromStep.forEach). Additionally, remindersToUpdate.forEach(async (reminder) => { ... }) is explicitly an async forEach that is never awaited. This can leave reminders not cancelled/deleted while the router continues to delete/update workflows/steps (wrong behavior).

packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleEmailReminders.ts: handler(): cancels SendGrid scheduled sends with body { batch_id: reminder.referenceId, status: 'cancel' } for reminders where referenceId can be null (no null check). This will send an invalid request to SendGrid and can throw; because the whole loop is inside one try/catch, a single null/invalid referenceId can prevent deletion of other cancelled reminders in that run.

packages/features/ee/workflows/lib/reminders/emailReminderManager.ts: deleteScheduledEmailReminder(): when referenceId is present and immediateDelete is falsy/omitted, it only updates the DB (sets cancelled: true) and does NOT cancel the SendGrid scheduled send. Call sites in handleCancelBooking.ts and bookings.tsx pass (reminder.id, reminder.referenceId) without immediateDelete, so scheduled emails can still be sent even though the reminder is marked cancelled (behavioral bug).
phase4_final: packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts: handler(): uses booking.workflowReminders.forEach(...) to call async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder but never awaits the returned promises. The function then proceeds to await only attendeeDeletes/bookingReferenceDeletes/apiDeletes, so reminder cancellation/deletion can be dropped (wrong behavior, especially if the process/request ends early).

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx: bookingsRouter (reschedule path): bookingToReschedule.workflowReminders.forEach(...) calls async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting. The previous code awaited Promise.all(remindersToDelete); now nothing is awaited, so reminders may not be cancelled/deleted before the mutation continues/returns.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/workflows.tsx: workflowsRouter: multiple reminder-cancellation loops call async deleteScheduledEmailReminder/deleteScheduledSMSReminder without awaiting (e.g., scheduledReminders.forEach, remindersToDelete.flat().forEach, remindersFromStep.forEach). Additionally, remindersToUpdate.forEach(async (reminder) => { ... }) is explicitly an async forEach that is never awaited. This can leave reminders not cancelled/deleted while the router continues to delete/update workflows/steps (wrong behavior).

packages/features/ee/workflows/api/scheduleEmailReminders.ts: handler(): cancels SendGrid scheduled sends with body { batch_id: reminder.referenceId, status: 'cancel' } for reminders where referenceId can be null (no null check). This will send an invalid request to SendGrid and can throw; because the whole loop is inside one try/catch, a single null/invalid referenceId can prevent deletion of other cancelled reminders in that run.

packages/features/ee/workflows/lib/reminders/emailReminderManager.ts: deleteScheduledEmailReminder(): when referenceId is present and immediateDelete is falsy/omitted, it only updates the DB (sets cancelled: true) and does NOT cancel the SendGrid scheduled send. Call sites in handleCancelBooking.ts and bookings.tsx pass (reminder.id, reminder.referenceId) without immediateDelete, so scheduled emails can still be sent even though the reminder is marked cancelled (behavioral bug).

[s=2] Logic bug: `isTeamAdminOrOwner` uses `isTeamAdmin(...) && isTeamOwner(...)`. That requires the user to be BOTH admin and owner, which will incorrectly deny access to legitimate team admins (or owners)

[s=2] Case-sensitivity bug: duplicate detection compares `guest === attendee.email` without normalizing case. This allows adding the same email with different casing (e.g., `A@x.com` vs `a@x.com`), creating

[s=2] Race condition / TOCTOU: uniqueness is checked against `booking.attendees` read earlier, then `createMany` is executed. Two concurrent addGuests requests can both pass the check and both insert the sa
phase4_verified_raw: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — authorization bug: `isTeamAdminOrOwner` is computed with `&&` between `isTeamAdmin(...)` and `isTeamOwner(...)`, so a user must be BOTH admin and owner to pass. Legit team admins (not owners) will be incorrectly rejected with FORBIDDEN.

packages/emails/email-manager.ts:sendAddGuestsEmails — attendee email selection is inverted: when `newGuests.includes(attendee.email)` it sends `AttendeeScheduledEmail` to newly added guests and `AttendeeAddGuestsEmail` to existing attendees. This results in wrong email template/subject being sent to the wrong recipients.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — API/UI contract mismatch: handler returns `{ message: "Guests added" }` (hardcoded English), but the UI error handling uses `t(err.message)` and elsewhere expects translation keys; this will produce non-localized/mismatched messaging behavior.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — case-sensitivity bug in blacklist check: `blacklistedGuestEmails` are lowercased, but `guest` is not lowercased before `blacklistedGuestEmails.includes(guest)`. Blacklisted emails can bypass by using different casing.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — case-sensitivity bug in duplicate attendee check: `!booking.attendees.some((attendee) => guest === attendee.email)` compares raw strings; same email with different casing will be treated as unique and can be added again, causing duplicate attendees/incorrect behavior.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — wrong recipient list passed to email sender: `sendAddGuestsEmails(evt, guests)` passes the original `guests` array, not `uniqueGuests`. If some inputs were already attendees/blacklisted, `sendAddGuestsEmails` will misclassify recipients (e.g., treat existing attendees as “new guests”) and send incorrect templates.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts:addGuestsHandler — error handling bug: the `catch` around `sendAddGuestsEmails` swallows the actual error (`console.log` only) and continues returning success. This will report “Guests added” even when email sending fails, causing incorrect user-visible behavior and making failures hard to diagnose.

This results in wrong email template/subject being sent to the wrong recipients.

phase4_verified_raw: apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/disable.ts handler: backup-code path validates membership but never consumes/removes the used code (comment explicitly says it won’t). It then disables 2FA by setting backupCodes: null, so the same backup code can be reused multiple times until 2FA is disabled, violating one-time-use semantics and enabling repeated disables if a code is leaked.

apps/web/components/settings/EnableTwoFactorModal.tsx EnableTwoFactorModal: creates an object URL (backupCodesUrl) via URL.createObjectURL but never revokes it on modal close/unmount/resetState; it only revokes when creating a new URL. Reopening/closing the modal can leak object URLs/memory in long-running sessions.

apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/disable.ts handler: req.body.backupCode is used as a string (req.body.backupCode.replaceAll) without type validation. If backupCode is non-string (e.g., number/object), this throws at runtime and returns 500 instead of a controlled 400.

packages/features/auth/lib/next-auth-options.ts CredentialsProvider authorize: backup code “consumption” sets backupCodes[index] = null and re-encrypts. This leaves null entries in the stored array (not removing the element), so the stored list never shrinks and accumulates nulls; it also makes the stored type inconsistent (strings + nulls), which can break assumptions elsewhere and is not a proper one-time-use removal.

packages/features/auth/lib/next-auth-options.ts CredentialsProvider authorize: backup code consumption is non-atomic (decrypt -> indexOf -> update). Two concurrent login requests using the same backup code can both pass the check before either update commits, allowing the same backup code to be used twice (TOCTOU race).

apps/web/components/auth/BackupCode.tsx: file is named/exported as BackupCode (default export) but the component function is named TwoFactor. This naming mismatch is a real maintainability/debugging bug (stack traces/tooling/auto-import confusion) and increases risk of importing the wrong TwoFactor component since apps/web/components/auth/TwoFactor.tsx also default-exports a function named TwoFactor.
phase4_final: apps/web/pages/api/auth/two-factor/totp/disable.ts handler: backup-code path validates membership but never consumes/removes the used code (comment explicitly says it won’t). Reopening/closing the modal can leak object URLs/memory in long-running sessions.

phase4_verified_raw: packages/core/EventManager.ts: In constructor/initialization logic, `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = evt.destinationCalendar ?? []; if (evt.location === MeetLocationType && mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration !== "google_calendar")` will throw when `evt.destinationCalendar` is null/empty because `mainHostDestinationCalendar` can be `undefined` and `.integration` is accessed without optional chaining.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: updateEvent() computes `selectedCalendar` as `externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` When `externalCalendarId` is undefined (common), the `.find()` compares against undefined and returns undefined, so the update targets the default calendar instead of the event’s destination calendar.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: deleteEvent() computes `calendarId` as `externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` When `externalCalendarId` is undefined, `.find()` returns undefined and deletion falls back to default calendarId ("primary"), potentially deleting from the wrong calendar or failing with 404.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: updateCalendarEvent() changes `calendarReference` to `PartialReference[] | undefined` but assigns via `newBooking?.references.length ? newBooking.references.filter(...) : booking.references.filter(...)`. If `newBooking` exists but `newBooking.references` is undefined, `newBooking?.references.length` evaluates to undefined and the else branch runs; if `booking.references` is undefined, `.filter` throws. Additionally, `if (calendarReference.length === 0)` will throw if `calendarReference` is still undefined.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: updateCalendarEvent() can call `updateEvent(credential, ...)` with `credential` undefined in the `reference.credentialId` branch: after failing to find in memory and DB, it still does `result.push(updateEvent(credential, event, ...))`, which will crash inside updateEvent/getCalendar when using the credential.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: createAllCalendarEvents() no-credentialId branch calls `createEvent(c, event)` without passing `destination.externalId`, so `EventResult.externalId` is not set for those created events; later code now relies on `result.externalId` for `externalCalendarId`, causing missing/incorrect externalCalendarId tracking for those events.

packages/app-store/larkcalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: createEvent/createAttendees/updateEvent/deleteEvent now use `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = event.destinationCalendar ?? [];` and then `mainHostDestinationCalendar?.externalId`. If `event.destinationCalendar` is `null` (allowed by type `DestinationCalendar[] | null`), `?? []` does not apply (nullish coalescing does apply to null, but here it is `?? []` so it does apply; however in other files they use `event.destinationCalendar ?? []` consistently—this one is safe). (No issue here)

packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts: `teamDestinationCalendars` is populated inside `users.slice(1).map(async ...)` by pushing into a shared array from concurrent async callbacks. Because the callbacks run concurrently, the order of pushes is nondeterministic, so `evt.destinationCalendar?.push(...teamDestinationCalendars)` can produce unstable destinationCalendar ordering (and the code elsewhere treats the first element as “main host”), leading to wrong “main host” selection depending on timing.

packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts: When persisting booking.destinationCalendar, it now always connects only `evt.destinationCalendar[0].id` even for COLLECTIVE where multiple destination calendars are added. This drops the additional destination calendars from being stored on the booking, so later flows that rebuild `evt` from `booking.destinationCalendar` will not have the full list and will not update/delete all created events.

packages/features/bookings/lib/handleNewBooking.ts: Typo fix introduces a new identifier `originalBookingDuration` but the previous misspelling `orginalBookingDuration` was used; this diff updates both declaration and usage, so it compiles. (No issue here)

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/requestReschedule.handler.ts: destinationCalendar assignment was `bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar || bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar` (redundant) and is now `bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar ? [bookingToReschedule?.destinationCalendar] : []`. This drops fallback to `bookingToReschedule.user.destinationCalendar` (used elsewhere), so reschedule requests for bookings without a destinationCalendar will now send an empty destinationCalendar array, potentially changing downstream behavior (e.g., Meet fallback logic and calendar selection).
phase4_final: packages/core/EventManager.ts: In constructor/initialization logic, `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = evt.destinationCalendar ?? []; if (evt.location === MeetLocationType && mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration !== "google_calendar")` will throw when `evt.destinationCalendar` is null/empty because `mainHostDestinationCalendar` can be `undefined` and `.integration` is accessed without optional chaining.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: updateEvent() computes `selectedCalendar` as `externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` When `externalCalendarId` is undefined (common), the `.find()` compares against undefined and returns undefined, so the update targets the default calendar instead of the event’s destination calendar.

packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: deleteEvent() computes `calendarId` as `externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` When `externalCalendarId` is undefined, `.find()` returns undefined and deletion falls back to default calendarId ("primary"), potentially deleting from the wrong calendar or failing with 404.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: updateCalendarEvent() changes `calendarReference` to `PartialReference[] | undefined` but assigns via `newBooking?.references.length ? newBooking.references.filter(...) : booking.references.filter(...)`. If `newBooking` exists but `newBooking.references` is undefined, `newBooking?.references.length` evaluates to undefined and the else branch runs; if `booking.references` is undefined, `.filter` throws. Additionally, `if (calendarReference.length === 0)` will throw if `calendarReference` is still undefined.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: updateCalendarEvent() can call `updateEvent(credential, ...)` with `credential` undefined in the `reference.credentialId` branch: after failing to find in memory and DB, it still does `result.push(updateEvent(credential, event, ...))`, which will crash inside updateEvent/getCalendar when using the credential.

packages/core/EventManager.ts: createAllCalendarEvents() no-credentialId branch calls `createEvent(c, event)` without passing `destination.externalId`, so `EventResult.externalId` is not set for those created events; later code now relies on `result.externalId` for `externalCalendarId`, causing missing/incorrect externalCalendarId tracking for those events.

packages/app-store/larkcalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts: createEvent/createAttendees/updateEvent/deleteEvent now use `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = event.destinationCalendar ?? [];` and then `mainHostDestinationCalendar?.externalId`. If `event.destinationCalendar` is `null` (allowed by type `DestinationCalendar[] | null`), `?? []` does not apply (nullish coalescing does apply to null, but here it is `?? []` so it does apply; however in other files they use `event.destinationCalendar ?? []` consistently—this one is safe). (No issue here)

phase4_verified_raw: packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts: Reschedule() now uses bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...) ... }), but the returned promises are never awaited. This means deleteEvent/deleteMeeting operations can still be running (and rejections can go unobserved) when Reschedule continues/returns, causing nondeterministic/incomplete cleanup.

packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts: Reschedule() now uses bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...) ... }), but the returned promises are never awaited. This can leave calendar/video deletions running after Reschedule continues/returns and can swallow failures.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx: bookingsRouter reschedule flow now uses bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...) ... }) without awaiting the resulting promises. Calendar/video deletions may not complete before the mutation returns, and errors may be unhandled.

packages/core/CalendarManager.ts: getCalendarCredentials() still does `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` after getCalendar was changed to async. This stores a Promise in `calendar` (and returns it in the integration objects), breaking any code that expects `calendar` to be a Calendar instance unless it is explicitly awaited everywhere.

packages/core/CalendarManager.ts: deleteEvent() was changed to `export const deleteEvent = async (...)` but its return type annotation remains `: Promise<unknown> => { ... }` while it can return `undefined` when no calendar is available (falls through without returning). This is a concrete API contract mismatch that can break callers relying on a Promise result.
phase4_final: packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts: Reschedule() now uses bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...) ... }), but the returned promises are never awaited. This means deleteEvent/deleteMeeting operations can still be running (and rejections can go unobserved) when Reschedule continues/returns, causing nondeterministic/incomplete cleanup.

packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx: bookingsRouter reschedule flow now uses bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... await getCalendar(...) ... }) without awaiting the resulting promises. Calendar/video deletions may not complete before the mutation returns, and errors may be unhandled.

packages/core/CalendarManager.ts: getCalendarCredentials() still does `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` after getCalendar was changed to async. This stores a Promise in `calendar` (and returns it in the integration objects), breaking any code that expects `calendar` to be a Calendar instance unless it is explicitly awaited everywhere.

packages/core/CalendarManager.ts: deleteEvent() was changed to `export const deleteEvent = async (...)` but its return type annotation remains `: Promise<unknown> => { ... }` while it can return `undefined` when no calendar is available (falls through without returning). This is a concrete API contract mismatch that can break callers relying on a Promise result.
